meta.method: 24 parallel auditors (14 area + 7 cross-cutting + 3 empirical) -> semantic dedup -> adversarial verification (1 skeptic per finding) -> completeness critic

area_summaries.sg-core: The sg-core area is in good shape overall. packages/abm_models/abm_models/sg/ contains the canonical Speculation Game: history.py (base-5 history encoder + Eq.6 quantizer), reference.py (per-agent dataclass loop), vectorized.py (mask/scatter NumPy port sharing the exact RNG consumption order), and __init__.py (SpeculationGame dataclass wrapper with backend dispatch and BASELINE_PARAMS). I diffed all three core files against the declared source of truth imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/{model,simulate,history}.py: they are byte-identical except for relative-import lines, so there is currently zero drift. I also re-verified reference-vs-vectorized bit-parity myself for the default, decision_mode='random', and history_mode='exogenous' paths, and confirmed the vectorized virtual open/close mask ordering cannot create same-step open-then-close artifacts (open sets v_pos=rec, and rec==-rec is impossible for rec!=0). The ABMModel protocol (base.py) is a minimal runtime-checkable "seed -> dict" contract; tests/test_registry_protocol.py instantiates all 8 REGISTRY models and checks isinstance + run, so protocol conformance is genuinely tested. The recorded metrics in tests/test_sg_parity.py match YH005 outputs/baseline_metrics.json exactly, and docs/backbone_parity.md's 0.00% claim is consistent with the byte-identical port. The main weaknesses are on the test/guard side, not in the model math: the workspace bit-parity test is a heavily stripped-down version of YH005's own parity suite (1 seed, 4 output keys, default modes only), the richer YH005 tests are not collected (testpaths = ["tests", "packages"]), and unlike the classical models there is no automated check that the packages/ copy stays identical to the YH005 originals. The AC2 findings-parity gate is slow-marked and excluded from the default suite. Minor silent-failure modes exist in the backend dispatch fallback and in returns_of's NaN substitution for non-positive prices. The known tie-break divergence aside, I found no correctness bug in either SG implementation.
area_summaries.classical-models: The classical-models area of packages/abm_models contains two lineages: (1) faithful literature reproductions promoted from the user's own YH001-YH004 experiments (cont_bouchaud, lux_marchesi, minority_game, gcmg), and (2) PRISM intervention-framework adapters (chiarella_iori, zero_intelligence, franke_westerhoff) plus _prism_compat.py holding the frozen dataclass/protocol types. I diffed every package copy against its imported original: all seven are byte-identical modulo the import line, so the "verbatim extraction / bit-identical parity" claims in docs/backbone_parity.md hold, and tests/test_classical_parity.py + test_prism_family_parity.py genuinely enforce that. The YH-lineage models are scientifically solid: Cont-Bouchaud's vectorized ER-graph cluster generation is exactly G(n,p) and its upper-triangle index arithmetic is correct (brute-force verified); Lux-Marchesi's opinion/strategy transition probabilities, U1/U2 forcing terms, and discrete-tick price dynamics match the 2000 IJTAF equations; the MG and GCMG scoring, history encoding, and rolling signed-score windows match Challet-Zhang and Jefferies et al. However, the minority_game docstring makes a false, empirically disproven claim that the class-based reference and vectorized versions consume RNG identically and match under the same seed (they diverge at step 0), and the GCMG binomial-theory helper uses a strict-inequality CDF inconsistent with the simulator's activity rule (confirmed numerically). The PRISM-lineage adapters are the weak flank: they are stylized caricatures whose docstrings and describe_complexity() descriptions claim literature mechanisms (CDA order book for CI, budget-constrained ZI-C for ZI, FW-2012 DCA/TPA switching for FW) that the code simply does not implement, and the CI transaction-tax dampening is dimensionally inconsistent with FW's (weaker by a factor of ~price, i.e., ~100x), making that intervention a numerical no-op. All adapters also retain PRISM's dangerous default of averaging returns across paths when n_paths>1 (destroying kurtosis/vol facts), a pitfall PRISM's own pipeline explicitly worked around; in-repo callers currently always pass n_paths=1. Reproducibility is otherwise good: every model seeds np.random.default_rng locally with no global RNG or hidden env coupling. The main testing gap is that all classical-model tests are copy-parity tests, which are tautological with respect to scientific correctness — no behavioral test checks the MG sigma^2/N curve, LM stationarity near z-bar, CB cluster power law, or GCMG activity levels.
area_summaries.stylized-facts: I audited the statistical-analysis layer: packages/stylized_facts/stylized_facts/core.py (the canonical extracted copy, byte-identical to imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/analysis.py), the legacy imported/speculation-game-info/analysis/{stylized_facts,tail_exponent,volatility_clustering}.py stack, the YH006/YH006_1 analysis code, and YH005 null_tests/compare scripts. The core estimators (log returns with p<=0 NaN masking, ACF with NaN-aware pair products, Hill MLE, windowed excess kurtosis, CCDF) are structurally sound: I numerically validated the ACF against an AR(1) ground truth and the Hill estimator against exact Pareto(alpha=3) samples, and both recover the truth well. Data flow is: simulate() -> log_returns_from_prices -> stylized_facts_summary (ret/vol ACF, windowed kurtosis, Hill alpha) -> metrics JSON, plus a set of Phase-1 mechanism figure functions (wealth CCDF, round-trip horizon, deltaG-vs-horizon, hold ratio, order-size decomposition) that also emit metrics. The assigned YH006/analysis/*.py files turned out to be 0-byte stubs; worse, that empty local `analysis` package shadows YH005's analysis.py on sys.path, so YH006/run_aggregate_c0.py fails at import (verified by execution) and its metrics JSONs indeed do not exist in the repo. The single most serious defect is in the active YH006_1 line: bin_variance_slope computes Var(log|dG|) with an arbitrary 1e-9 floor, but dG is an int64 cognitive P&L with 21% exact zeros in the researcher's own stored round-trip parquet; each zero becomes log(1e-9) = -20.7, so bin variance is dominated by the per-bin zero-fraction, and re-computing on the stored data flips the headline indicator from -0.20 (as reported throughout the YH006_1 README, driving the 'microstructure true effect' conclusion) to +0.98 once zeros are excluded. A second measurable defect: plot_hold_ratio estimates N as the max step-sum of four action counts; the recorded phase1_metrics.json shows N_est=983 for an N=1000 run, so all published action-ratio fractions are inflated ~1.7% even though the exact N is available as len(sim_result['final_wealth']). Smaller issues include a nonstandard ACF normalization (numerator averaged over n-lag pairs, denominator over n, inflating large-lag values by n/(n-lag)), a one-step misalignment between the log-return and dp modes of the order-size figure, a mathematically wrong docstring claim (alpha<3 implies infinite variance), duplicate stylized-facts implementations with conflicting kurtosis conventions (raw vs excess), and a testing gap: the estimator suite is validated only by a slow-marked parity test that uses the same implementation on both sides, so it is circular and excluded from the default 74-test run. Overall health: the canonical estimator core is correct, but the active YH006_1 heteroscedasticity pipeline and the YH006 reproduction path both contain result-changing defects.
area_summaries.fingerprint-atlas: packages/fingerprint_atlas is a self-contained "research copilot" pipeline: fingerprint.py turns a return series into a 9-D stylized-facts vector (Hill tail, ACF long-memory axes, aggregational-kurtosis decay); adapters.py + populate.py LHS-sample the 8 abm_models and write fingerprinted runs into a SQLite `runs` table (db.py); synthetic.py/real_refs.py/populate_refs.py add GARCH/Levy/regime probes and Yahoo-Finance real-market landmarks; atlas.py computes a separation validation gate + PCA plots. On top sits an LLM loop: arxiv_ingest.py pulls papers and has Groq extract structured mechanism fields into `literature_methods`; propose.py builds a corpus context and asks Groq for parameter-sweep proposals (validated against MODEL_BOUNDS and a Japanese-rationale quality filter); propose_cli.py executes accepted proposals, measures actual fingerprints and records prediction_error/novelty for the analytics.py learning curve; preference.py/label_cli.py implement a ridge+UCB human-preference labeling loop; methods.py/methods_draft.py/methods_cli.py hold the user's methodology commentary with LLM drafting. Overall code quality is good — parameterized SQL throughout (no injection surface found), idempotent additive schema migrations, seeded RNGs for all simulation sampling, defensive LLM-output coercion, and dry-run hooks that let tests exercise parse/validate/store paths without network. The main weaknesses are concentrated in the newest, least-tested seams: execute_proposal misaligns its index between the unfiltered run list and the NaN-filtered fingerprint matrix (the core LLM-calibration metric), the preference loop is not NaN-safe even though NaN fingerprints are an expected data condition, and the labeling CLI replays real-market windows against a freshly-fetched (shifted) series. The ingest path silently discards low-relevance extraction results (guaranteeing repeated Groq spend) and keeps arXiv version suffixes in the unique key despite a comment claiming otherwise. Docstrings drift (6-D vs 9-D vector, Llama vs gpt-oss default), and there is no provenance of prompt version/temperature for the LLM calls despite the monorepo having a provenance package. Tests cover the dry-run propose/store path, ingest coercion, methods and preference math, but not execute_proposal — the single most defect-dense function in the package.
area_summaries.infra: I audited the monorepo infrastructure harness: src/fabm, the two thin experiment wrappers, configs/, the provenance package, root pyproject, the three GitHub workflows, .gitignore, and the tests/ layout. The workspace is a uv workspace (packages/* as members) with pytest testpaths=[tests, packages]; the experiment wrappers (experiments/classical/baseline.py, experiments/speculation_game/baseline.py) are genuinely thin and import only canonical cores, matching spec 001 O1. However, the 'fabm' harness that README promises (config-driven experiments, multi-stream seeding, metrics tables) is dead scaffolding: config.py, configs/experiment_example.yaml, tests/test_config.py and tests/test_rng.py are all 0 bytes, and rng.py's make_rngs (which is itself correctly implemented via SeedSequence.spawn) is imported nowhere. CI runs uv sync + pytest -m "not slow", which means the SG statistical-parity go/no-go gate (the basis of docs/backbone_parity.md's 0.00% claim) never executes in CI, and the ruff lint step is neutered with '|| true'. The two scheduled LLM workflows (ingest_arxiv, propose) are structurally broken: both open PRs against a nonexistent ephemeral branch 'claude/vibrant-ptolemy-fapuxw', and propose.yml rebuilds the DB without restoring the literature snapshot that ingest_arxiv accumulates, so the advertised ingest-to-propose literature loop never closes; they also form a prompt-injection path from arbitrary arXiv abstracts into repo-committed content under contents:write permissions. The provenance package delivers only emit_prov (prov_record), not the PROV-O + L2 ctx integration spec 001 T1 promises, and its records are homemade JSON, not actual PROV-O. Test coverage of packages/ is good for abm_models (bit-parity for all 8 model families plus a protocol test) and minimal-but-present for provenance; stylized_facts has no dedicated tests at all (only reached via the slow-marked SG parity test), and fingerprint_atlas's real_refs/populate_refs/label_cli/analytics are untested. Overall: the canonical model cores and their parity tests are healthy, but the surrounding automation layer contains several claims that the code does not honor.
area_summaries.yh001-005: Audited imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH001–YH005 plus YH005_1: five paper-reproduction experiments (Cont-Bouchaud percolation, Lux-Marchesi, Minority Game, Grand-Canonical MG, and the Katahira Speculation Game) that culminate in YH005, the canonical SG that was later extracted verbatim (diff-confirmed, imports only) into packages/abm_models/sg. YH005 is in strong shape: model.py (per-agent reference) and simulate.py (vectorized) implement identical state machines, and I traced the §7 RNG-consumption contract call-by-call in both — init order (strategies, init_u100, init_active, mu0), per-agent random-mode draws, mid-step exogenous μ redraw, then index-ordered review rng.choice and 3-draw bankruptcy resampling — they match exactly, and tests/test_parity.py (18 cases) covers all modes; I confirmed the bankruptcy/review RNG paths are actually exercised at the test's N=30,T=300 scale (13–22 substitutions, ~1400–1700 closes per seed). The null-model tests are structurally fair (Null A keeps P real and only randomizes μ; Null B routes position-blind random recs through the shared effective-action table), and README numeric tables for baseline and null tests match the committed JSONs almost everywhere. The defects I found are concentrated at the reporting/analysis layer rather than the simulation core: plot_hold_ratio infers N from data and gets 983 instead of 1000, biasing every mechanism ratio quoted in YH005_1's README; YH005_1's own README table contradicts its committed phase1_metrics.json on four values; the committed null_tests_metrics.json is a stale pre-threshold-revision artifact whose recorded acceptance is "false"; and the YH005 README's 3-model-comparison narrative contains two factual errors (SG vol-ACF at τ=500, and a period-32 explanation for MG that my rerun disproves — the true cycle divides 8). Upstream, YH003's module docstring claims bit-parity between its reference and vectorized implementations "with the same seed", which I verified empirically to be false at t=0 (they use different tie-break RNG schemes and there is no parity test) — a claim-hygiene problem in a repo whose methodology leans on parity guarantees. YH001's simulation is fine but its README compares powerlaw's PDF exponent against a CCDF theoretical exponent, drawing the wrong conclusion from an excellent fit. Remaining items are research-rigor gaps a referee would raise: the "learning disabled" S=1 three-model comparison leaves SG's bankruptcy-substitution and wealth-sizing channels active, and the acceptance criteria ignore the significantly positive lag-1 return autocorrelation while a threshold was revised post-hoc (disclosed). Overall health: simulation cores are careful and well-tested where it matters most (YH005); documentation freshness and figure-level statistics are the weak layer.
area_summaries.yh006: YH006 ports the Katahira-Chen Speculation Game (SG) decision rule from the YH005 aggregate-demand world onto a PAMS 0.2.2 limit-order book: speculation_agent.py implements the SG agent (MARKET_ORDER open/close, next-step fill reconciliation, self-cancel of leftovers, opposing-liquidity guard, a 2-account design separating cognitive sg_wealth from PAMS LOB cash), history_broadcast.py provides the shared quinary cognitive history keyed per-simulator (correctly re-created per run, quantize/shift_in bit-identical to YH005/history.py), mm_fcn_agent.py supplies liquidity via a volume-scalable FCN clone, and yh006_to_yh005_adapter.py reshapes results into the YH005 dict schema. aggregate_sim.py is a local fork of YH005 simulate() adding Pareto wealth, protected by a genuinely strong bit-parity test (test_aggregate_parity.py, 4 seeds); run_aggregate_c0.py/compare_figure.py produce the 2x2 (world x wealth) comparison that is the README's headline result. The aggregate side is healthy. The LOB side has a load-bearing defect: reconciliation reads asset_volumes from a start-of-step snapshot while the previous step's leftover MARKET orders stay live until their Cancel is processed mid-step (pams collects all submit_orders first, then executes orders sequentially), and the close-reconcile classifies outcomes only by |actual_vol| vs entry_quantity with no sign or identity check. I instrumented and ran the actual C3 config in a sandbox (pams 0.2.2, seeds 777/42/1): 100/100 SG agents permanently desynchronize (median onset = first main-session step), ending with real inventories up to 15,529 shares while believing themselves near-flat — so recorded round trips, sg_wealth, and the LOB-side price formation feeding the README's 2x2 interaction claims are corrupted. Secondary issues: partial-close P&L is silently dropped from G and sg_wealth; README documents a LIMIT_ORDER mitigation that exists nowhere in code; SPEC's wealth semantics and run sizes drifted from the implementation; the wealth-conservation test is tautological and the round-trip dG test tolerates 50% mismatch (which is plausibly why the desync went unnoticed); an empty analysis/ stub package shadows YH005's analysis module so run_aggregate_c0.py imports work only via an accidental sys.path side effect in aggregate_sim.py. Reproducibility plumbing (seeded prng, per-simulator history state, parity tests) is otherwise well done.
area_summaries.yh006_1: YH006_1 is the Phase-2 ensemble/ablation stage of the user's Speculation Game LOB research: code/ contains a per-trial runner (run_experiment.py) that drives the Phase-1 aggregate sim (YH006/aggregate_sim.py) and PAMS LOB sessions via read-only agent subclasses (sg_agent.py), an adapter to 4 parquet schemas (adapter.py), a trial-level multiprocessing wrapper (parallel.py), per-stage ensemble/aggregation scripts (S2 aggregate_ensemble, S3 lob_ensemble+combine+aggregate_full_summary, S4/S5 q_const calibration + A1 ablation, S5.5 subsample, S5.6 MMFCN scan, S5.7/S5.8 Kaplan-Meier survival, S6 tau_max + A3 runner), and shared stats helpers (stats.py, analysis.py). Reproducibility engineering is strong: every trial is independently seeded (1000–1099), determinism guards (2x independent runs, sha256 + semantic compare) exist for every new code path, cross-machine integrity checks verify transferred parquet, and runtime assertions cross-check S5.5/S5.7 censoring rates. The survival machinery is correct: I verified km_from_counts against a hand-computed KM example, the T=1500/T=10000 re-censoring convention is consistent across S5.5/S5.7/S5.8, censored samples are properly kept at risk, and the trial-level multinomial cluster bootstrap plus the rule-of-three zero-event hazard bound in S5.8 are sound. The single biggest problem is numerical: the headline funnel metric bin_var_slope computes Var(log|ΔG|) with zeros clamped to 1e-9, and ~21% of round trips have ΔG==0, so the metric's sign (−0.40 for C0u) is driven entirely by the horizon-trend of P(ΔG=0); recomputing pooled slopes with zeros excluded flips every condition to ≈+1.0, contradicting the "heteroscedasticity funnel" interpretation that underpins the S3 2x2 pattern, the S5.5 H_micro verdict thresholds, and the S5 "hypothesis A revised" narrative. Statistically, the L2 shrinkage judgment divides two interaction estimates whose baseline (S3) 95% CIs all straddle zero, so ratio-based conclusions ("Spearman ratio 1.006 refutes hypothesis A") over-read noise; SPECv2.0's mandated Bonferroni correction (α=0.0125), Mann-Whitney tests, and independent-resample interaction bootstrap were never implemented (stats.py's two spec'd functions are dead code), and the actual interaction CIs use positional paired-difference bootstrapping that silently misaligns seeds if any condition has a NaN/missing trial (latent today — I verified all 600 rows are complete and seed-sorted). Docs are partially stale: OVERVIEW.md still says the ablations have not been run, while README/data contain the completed S4–S5.8 results. Testing is the other gap: the YH006_1 statistical core (KM, bootstrap, re-censoring, w_open/w_close and RNG-replay w_init reconstruction) has no unit tests of its own — only Phase-1 YH006/tests exist. Overall health: engineering discipline (seeding, guards, pre-registered verdicts, stop triggers) is well above typical research-code standards, but two statistical-interpretation defects (zero-inflated variance metric, noise-ratio ablation verdicts) sit directly under the stage's headline conclusions.
area_summaries.yh008-docs: This area is the documentation-and-frontier layer of the user's own research line: YH008 (LLM-as-agent mech-interp experiment: src pipeline probing Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct for disposition/ATH phenotypes via a group-normalized P(sell) logit read, plus 3 archived runs with gate reports), the YH007 skeleton README, the knowledge docs (findings.md, hypotheses.md, literature.md, YH006 research dossier, two refs walkthroughs), and the browser playground (Pyodide HTML pages + sf.py helpers + offline _selftest.py). The YH008 pipeline itself is unusually disciplined for research code: pinned model revision, frozen token groups, exploration/held-out split fixed before analysis, bootstrap-K=1000 CIs, determinism and batch-equivalence self-checks, and report numbers that I verified match the archived metrics.json/gate.json exactly. The two real weaknesses are methodological: (1) the behavioral "faithfulness" free generations bypass the chat template entirely (raw text, no BOS, into an Instruct model), so the archived generations are base-LM ramblings — yet REPORT.md uses them to buttress fixed research decision #3; and (2) the Stage-0.5 gate rule was revised after v1 data, and the P0.5 "CONFIRMED" verdict re-tests the same 113 exploration loss pairs with 3 correlated wordings while dismissing a genuine sign flip in the behavioral framing with a mechanism claim ("reason-generation artifact") that contradicts the measurement's own no-generation design. The knowledge docs have real drift: findings.md still presents the YH006 interaction −0.27 and the 81.1%-vs-0.9% censoring gap as confirmed primary evidence, while the (newer) YH006 dossier explicitly retires both (interaction null in ensemble; censoring comparison horizon-confounded); hypotheses.md/literature.md describe an abandoned Brock-Hommes program referencing nonexistent experiment dirs; and the refs code-semantics doc misstates the strategy tie-break as fully random when the code keeps the incumbent — exactly the semantics of the known Java/Python tie-break divergence. YH008's own README still claims "skeleton, unimplemented" despite src/ and three completed runs living beside it. The playground is healthy: _selftest.py runs the real models and passes with the project venv, and sf.py estimators verifiably match the YH005 analysis code they claim to mirror; the only gap is that the selftest is wired into nothing. Overall: computation trustworthy, statistics mostly careful, but the narrative layer (reports, findings.md, READMEs) lags or overstates the code in several load-bearing places.
area_summaries.prism: imported/PRISM/ is a retired "natural-experiment scoring" framework: five toy ABM adapters (sg/ci/zi/fw/lm) implementing a common ModelAdapter protocol (calibrate_baseline → apply_intervention → simulate), a stylized-fact estimator registry (facts/estimators.py), an empirical DiD module (empirical/did.py + data/jpx_data.py fetching yfinance log-returns), and a scoring stack (sign/magnitude match, MDL parameter-count weighting, causal-method weighting, eligibility gate) orchestrated by pipeline.py with W3C-PROV-style provenance sealing. The repo is unusually self-aware: docs/AUDIT_REPORT.md documents 12 defects (5 FATAL) and docs/FINAL_REPORT.md honestly concludes 0 of 120 cells are scientifically conclusive; several FATALs (per-path fact computation, CI95 zero-crossing → INCONCLUSIVE, ratio-based tick scaling, external_claim NER rejection) were genuinely fixed in code. However, the fixes were applied at the pipeline layer while the adapters themselves retain the known-broken `simulate(n_paths>1)` path-averaging, and exactly these adapters (ci.py, zi.py, fw.py) were extracted byte-identically into packages/abm_models/{chiarella_iori,zero_intelligence,franke_westerhoff}/model.py, so their defects now live in the canonical package and feed the active fingerprint-atlas runs (12 runs each in notebooks/atlas_v*/gate.json). The worst live defect is the CI adapter's transaction-tax term, which is a verified bit-identical no-op due to a double unit error plus tick rounding, contradicted by both the docstring and FINAL_REPORT's smuggling-audit table. The DiD module has a paired-bootstrap alignment bug (pre/post stock lists filtered independently, then paired by array position), and the estimator-level bootstrap CIs for autocorrelation-type facts use iid resampling that destroys the very dependence being measured. Cross-adapter intervention semantics are incoherent (a 0.3% tax means demand×0.99997 in CI, demand×0.997 in FW, and tick×30 in ZI/SG). Calibration is cosmetic (only first-moment targeting, with per-adapter hardcoded price_impact and in-place mutation of self.params). Tests are extensive (294 claimed) but intervention tests only assert parameter assignment, never behavioral effect, which is why the tax no-op is invisible. Overall: as a retired lineage the science was correctly self-invalidated, but the verbatim extraction into packages carried documented-defective code under canonical literature model names into active research.
area_summaries.prov-atlas-llm: Audited two imported repos. (1) imported/PROV-ABM-atlas/: a well-disciplined toy-first research repo pairing a provenance framework (provabm/: ctx capture, reported-reach, claim-vs-reach validator, L2 prov.json — all coherent and internally consistent with its CLAUDE.md refund table) with a mechanism-discrimination toy (toy/: single-asset excess-demand market, T/H agent populations, SF1-6 battery, LR/CNN/XGB classifiers, channel_band batch-auction experiment, minimal ZI order book). atlas/protocols.py is honestly a type-only scaffold as documented. Data flow: runners (run_one, equivalence_check, band_demo, band_analyses) → toy simulators → sf_battery features → classifiers → findings docs (0001 retracted and correctly re-interpreted, 0002 order-book decoupling probe). Overall hygiene is high (seeded RNG everywhere, deterministic tests, honest retraction of Finding 0001), but the two headline measuring instruments are statistically compromised: the 1D-CNN CV routine selects the best epoch on the test fold (upward-biased accuracy, feeding the 0.94 identification claims and the pre-registered equivalence/Null-1 gates), and the band experiments reuse identical seeds for both classes without grouped CV (producing the below-chance 0.33/0.38/0.46 accuracies that the docs report as "indistinguishable ≈0.5"). (2) imported/agent-based-modeling/: an LLM semantic-Schelling snapshot — src/semantic_schelling.py (embedding-similarity Schelling with Ollama-generated persona texts), src/backends/ (async Ollama/vLLM Stay-Move decision backends), experiments/poc_llm_decision/ (run.py v1 sync, run_v2.py async, run_mini.py, plus committed output artifacts). This code is PoC-grade: the v2 shared decision parser regressed to a substring match biased toward "Move", backend errors are silently coerced to "Stay" (an outage looks like equilibrium), LLM calls in v2 carry no seed despite results.json recording seed=42, and a falsy-zero bug mislabels the committed initial-state plot as "final". None of this touches the canonical packages/ or the active YH research line, so nothing rates critical, but the CNN leakage and seed-pairing issues materially affect numbers quoted in PROV-ABM-atlas findings/working-paper docs.
area_summaries.market-dynamics: imported/market-dynamics/state_atlas is a self-contained "Market State Atlas" pipeline: yfinance OHLCV (data/) -> causal rolling-z features (features/build.py) -> CPU beta-VAE 2D embedding (embedding/vae.py) -> Gaussian-KDE free-energy surface F=-log(rho) with basin/persistence machinery (density/free_energy.py) -> latent Kramers-Moyal/early-warning diagnostics (dynamics/) -> two evaluation layers: a Phase-7 single-split ANOVA-vs-permutation backtest (backtest/walkforward.py) and a Phase-8 online state machine + vol-carry strategy walk-forward (online/). pipeline.py and cli.py orchestrate; experiments/universe_comparison.py runs the pipeline per universe. The causal-feature discipline is genuinely strong: build_features uses past-only rolling windows, and tests include real future-corruption leakage canaries for both features and the online monitor; the VAE train/val split is chronological and OOS projection is deterministic (mu only). Overall code quality is high with unusually honest docstrings about uncalibrated quantities (Kramers lifetimes, KDE noise). The defects found are concentrated at the edges: a confirmed crash in the basin dwell counter (broadcast bug) on the main `atlas` path, state-machine run-length counters that reset at every walk-forward window boundary (systematically disarming the persistent-stress kill-switch exactly on the dates whose weights are kept), an ANOVA/permutation test whose null ignores the heavy autocorrelation of overlapping 21-day forward-vol windows, features built on unadjusted close despite fetching adj_close, and a data path where a partial yfinance failure can silently cache an empty frame. Reproducibility is mostly good (seeded torch generator, cache keyed by date) but the VAE seeds the global torch RNG as a constructor side effect, and same-day cache hits can freeze an intraday partial bar. The spec ground truth (specs/001, T6 "regimes 統合") says this code will become packages/regimes; that extraction has not happened, so this imported copy is currently the only implementation. Test coverage is good for features/density/online-monitor but zero for the money-simulation layer (online/backtest.py).
area_summaries.abm-microstructure: imported/ABM-Microstructure is a two-experiment market-microstructure harness: Experiment A (001) is a vectorized single-pass simulator (engine.py + config.py + metrics.py) of a jump-driven true value with a stale-quoting MM, one monopolist sniper, and undirected noise flow, validated against four independently implemented analytical anchors (anchors.py: GM break-even, exact binomial Budish sniping rent, identity-blind Kyle lambda, uniform-price clearing in book.py). Experiment B (002) is a separate sequential learning environment (env.py) with n tabular Q/SARSA market makers (qlearn.py), mechanism-specific myopic-Nash/ZI/monopoly benchmarks (benchmarks.py), a collusion certification gate (verdict.py: measure -> impulse_response -> certify), a tiered design-map runner with an append-only budget ledger (designmap.py, scripts/run_design_map.py), and a BCS 2015 ES-SPY calibration registry (calibrations.py). Data flow: LearnConfig -> derive_rngs (deterministic per-purpose integer seeds) -> train -> frozen-policy measure on a separate env stream -> fully deterministic impulse-response rollout scored by analytic stage payoffs -> certify -> DesignMapPoint appended to CSV keyed by config_hash. Overall health is high: RNG discipline is exemplary (no global RNG, purpose-separated streams, bit-reproducibility tested), anchors are structurally independent of the sim, tests are adversarial rather than tautological, and the harness's known failures are documented as findings. Both 2026-06-11 incidents are verifiably fixed in code: 0002a (tier-3 key collision) via config_hash on DesignMapPoint + hash-based resume with a uniqueness guard for legacy CSVs (designmap.py:46-55, 495-508; regression test test_designmap.py:126-139), and 0002b (ledger snapshot lost update) via lock-file serialization plus an append-only journal with rebuild_spent/verify (designmap.py:119-176; regression tests test_designmap.py:54-87). The load-bearing defects that remain are concentrated in the Experiment-B gate logic: the v2 convergence detector is phase-sensitive to limit cycles, and the impulse-response gate assumes a fixed-point baseline that the v2 criterion explicitly does not guarantee; the batch-vs-continuous learning comparison also carries an unacknowledged reward-density confound. Smaller items are comment/spec drift and a tie-break bug in the unused clearing verifier.
area_summaries.notebooks: The notebooks/ directory contains no runnable notebooks at all: 00_sanity_check.jpynb is a 0-byte file (misspelled extension, empty since first_commit, referenced nowhere), and atlas_v1..v4 + atlas_v3_periods each hold only three committed output artifacts (atlas.png, features.png, gate.json) produced by the packages/fingerprint_atlas pipeline (populate.py -> db.py -> atlas.py). The generating SQLite DB is not in the repo and the exact CLI invocations/seeds live only in commit messages (f014e57, bce0b8f, 0e49cb7), so none of the committed atlas results can be regenerated or verified from the tree. Reading the artifacts against the pipeline code surfaced real numerics problems: atlas_v3/gate.json is stamped ok:true even though its own feature_means_raw show volatility mean 5.5e8 / std 6.1e9 — a non-stationary GARCH draw (alpha+beta>1, admitted in the v4 commit message) collapsed the standardized volatility axis to a single-run indicator (nearly every model centroid identical at -0.0894), meaning the v3 "instrument-independence" conclusions were computed on corrupted geometry while the gate certified it. The gate criterion in atlas.py (sil>0 and inter>intra) has no divergence/degeneracy guard, which is exactly why it passed. A second geometry bias is visible directly in the committed v4 centroids: minority_game, gcmg, and chiarella_iori sit at an identical 1.558 on the hill axis because fingerprint() imputes HILL_ALPHA_CAP=20.0 (a "maximally thin tail" verdict) whenever compute_hill=False, instead of NaN which distance_matrix already handles. The synthetic GARCH bounds were fixed in code (alpha+beta <= 0.99), but the poisoned v3/v3_periods artifacts remain committed with no in-tree explanation. Reproducibility of the real-market reference rows is structurally weak: real_refs.py anchors windows to time.time() and the weekly propose workflow rebuilds the DB from live Yahoo data with a throwaway cache dir under notebooks/. Stale pre-monorepo paths (../test/knowhow/abm_knowhow.db) persist in populate.py's docstring and the CI PR-body instructions, and the workflow's PR base is hardcoded to a session-named branch. The YH006 CI2002.ipynb is a verbatim, properly seeded (random.Random(42)) copy of the upstream pams example notebook — valid nbformat-4 JSON, low risk, though its execution counts (7..12) show the committed outputs did not come from a fresh top-to-bottom run. Overall: the pipeline code itself is decently seeded and structured, but the committed notebook-directory artifacts overstate validation status and are not reproducible from the repository.
area_summaries.sg-drift: Cross-implementation drift audit of all Speculation Game (SG) variants. Drift matrix vs the dissertation Java reference (imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/papers/katahira_dissertation_code/Sample.java), checked dimension-by-dimension: (1) decision rule / effective-action table (idle, open, passive-hold on rec==pos, active-hold on rec==0, close on rec==-pos, close reuses opening quantity): IDENTICAL in YH005 model.py, YH005 simulate.py, packages/abm_models/sg/{reference,vectorized}.py, YH006 aggregate_sim.py, and YH006 speculation_agent.py (LOB, modulo fills). (2) Quantization boundaries (dp==±C maps to ∓1, dp==0 to 0) and base-5 history-mu update order (snapshot mu used for decisions, shift_in after clearing): IDENTICAL everywhere; the two packages/sg files are byte-identical to YH005 except the relative import, so the ADR-0001/backbone_parity claims are structurally sound, and YH006/aggregate_sim.py is a bit-parity-tested fork adding only Pareto init. (3) Cognitive-price timing: Java and all aggregate Pythons mark entry/exit at post-update P (own-step impact included); YH006 LOB marks at submit-time P which reflects mid(t-1) — a real, undocumented semantic shift (finding). (4) Wealth update (w += dG*q_entry) and bankruptcy threshold (w<B at close, replaced same step, new w = B+U{0..99}): identical across all; only distribution-neutral Java quirks differ (init use=0, replace keeps use, review skipped when bankrupt) — reported once as info. (5) Strategy-review tie-break: all five Python implementations agree with each other (incumbent keeps seat when tied-best; YH006 uses prng.randrange(len(best)) but same semantics), so no Python-vs-Python tie-break drift exists beyond the known Java coin-flip divergence. (6) Virtual-trade bookkeeping (open on rec!=0, hold on rec==vpos or 0, close on rec==-vpos, per-unit G, new active's virtual killed on switch without G update): identical everywhere. (7) The one substantive behavioral bug found is in the active YH006 LOB line: partial closes silently drop the realized P&L of the filled fraction from sg_wealth and G, and YH006_1's Phase-2 subclasses inherit it. (8) PRISM adapters/sg.py is CONFIRMED to be a Franke-Westerhoff-style fundamentalist/chartist/noise softmax model, not the SG, despite its docstring and PRISM README both citing "Katahira et al. (2019)"; it is byte-identical to imported/PROV-ABM-atlas/toy/models/sg.py and is imported only inside PRISM's own pipeline/tests and the PROV-ABM-atlas toy — fingerprint_atlas builds the real SG via abm_models.REGISTRY, and abm_models/sg/__init__.py explicitly warns about the name clash. Overall health: the aggregate-world SG family is in excellent shape (verbatim copies + bit-parity tests); the LOB port carries a real accounting defect plus undocumented timing semantics, and the only Java-fidelity guarantee is manual code reading — no automated golden-trace test against the Java reference exists.
area_summaries.rng-repro: I audited RNG and reproducibility discipline across packages/, src/, experiments/, imported/speculation-game-info/, imported/market-dynamics/, and imported/PROV-ABM-atlas/ (PRISM and ABM-Microstructure excluded per assignment). Overall seeding discipline in the canonical cores is good: packages/abm_models models take an explicit `run(seed)` and thread a single `np.random.default_rng(seed)` with documented consumption order; the SG vectorized/reference pair deliberately preserves per-agent RNG consumption order for bit-parity, and YH006_1 has real determinism guards (sha256 of parquet from two same-seed runs). The YH006_1 multiprocessing wrapper (code/parallel.py) is deterministic by construction — every trial is fully seeded, outputs are keyed by seed, and imap_unordered ordering only affects logs — so parallelism does not break determinism. The real problems are downstream in the analysis layer of the active research line (YH006_1): trial-level "paired" statistics align conditions positionally after dropna()/min-size truncation instead of merging on seed, worker errors returned by run_parallel_trials are discarded by three of five ensemble drivers, and missing trial parquets are silently skipped during aggregation — together these can silently shrink and misalign the 100-trial ensembles that feed the headline interaction CIs. Bootstrap RNG hygiene is sloppy but deterministic: a hardcoded default_rng(0) default in stats.bootstrap_ci is re-instantiated all over YH006_1 and YH008 reuses one bseed across metric families, so resample-index matrices repeat across supposedly independent CIs. The workspace-level rng scaffold is dead: src/fabm/rng.py's make_rngs (the SeedSequence.spawn discipline the README advertises) is imported nowhere, and tests/test_rng.py plus test_config.py are 0-byte files inside the passing suite. The canonical CI/ZI/FW extractions retain PRISM's `default_rng(seed+i)` consecutive-seed path streams plus cross-path return averaging — latent because run() uses n_paths=1, but a trap for any future ensemble use. The provenance core package records no seed, git commit, or config hash, contradicting the monorepo's own reproducibility table spec. PROV-ABM-atlas's fast kernel seeds the global legacy np.random inside an njit function, which mutates interpreter-global RNG state whenever JIT is disabled (as in its CI). Finally, the w_init RNG-replay logic exists in three copies coupled to simulate_aggregate's exact draw order, with the Pareto branch never verified by a test.
area_summaries.test-quality: I audited test quality repo-wide: the 13 workspace files under tests/ (73 fast tests + 1 slow, all passing when I ran them), the YH005 and YH006 experiment suites under imported/speculation-game-info, package-level tests (none exist), and the CI wiring in .github/workflows/ci.yml. The workspace suite is strongest on the fingerprint_atlas LLM-pipeline side (test_propose.py, test_preference.py, test_methods.py, test_arxiv_ingest.py assert real behavior with dry-run seams, including a genuine closed-loop acceptance test of the active-learning contract). The simulation side is much weaker: every 'parity' test is self-referential — classical/PRISM parity compares the packages/ copy against the imported/ copy of the same source, and the SG 'findings parity' compares against baseline_metrics.json that the same Python lineage generated (hence the documented 0.00% error) — so no test anchors any model to independent ground truth such as the dissertation Java reference. The single quantitative SG validation is slow-marked and CI runs `pytest -m "not slow"`, so nothing in automation guarantees it still passes (it does pass locally today, ~5 min). The stylized_facts package — the shared measurement library every parity claim flows through — has zero unit tests; its Hill estimator, ACF, and windowed kurtosis are exercised only via that slow-excluded test. The active research line YH006 is worse: its four test files never run in CI, three of four cannot even be collected in the monorepo environment because pams==0.2.2 is not in the uv workspace, its 'wealth conservation' test bottoms out in `assert isinstance(sum_dG_q, (int, float))` (a tautology), and its delta_G consistency check tolerates a 50% mismatch rate. The load-bearing YH006 _reconcile edge cases (zero-fill open, partial open/close, close-cancel, bankruptcy substitution) have no direct tests at all, and the known tie-break divergence is not pinned by any spec-level test. Two workspace test files (test_rng.py, test_config.py) are 0-byte stubs matching untested src/fabm modules, and the CI lint step is neutered with `|| true`. Overall health: good hygiene and real assertions on the atlas/LLM tooling; a self-parity monoculture plus CI exclusions on exactly the simulation code the research conclusions depend on.
area_summaries.stats-method: I audited the statistical-methodology layer of the monorepo: the canonical stylized-facts estimators (packages/stylized_facts/stylized_facts/core.py, bit-identical to imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/analysis.py), the older analysis helpers (imported/speculation-game-info/analysis/*.py), the YH005 evidence scripts (null_tests.py, compare_three_models.py), the YH006_1 Phase-2 pipeline (analysis.py, stats.py, aggregate_ensemble.py, aggregate_full_summary.py, aggregate_mmfcn_sensitivity.py, subsample_aggregate.py, survival_analysis.py), and the fingerprint_atlas stylized-facts vector (fingerprint.py). Data flow: simulations write per-seed parquet (round trips, agents, lifetimes, wealth snapshots); trial-level metrics (correlations, quantile-regression slopes, bin-variance slope, Hill alpha, lifetime stats) are aggregated into ensemble summaries with 10k-resample bootstrap CIs; headline claims rest on a 2x2 world-by-wealth interaction and on a Kaplan-Meier survival comparison. Overall health is mixed-good: the YH006_1 pipeline shows real statistical sophistication (seed-clustered multinomial bootstrap for KM curves, Greenwood-vs-cluster-bootstrap width comparison, determinism guards, censoring-consistency assertions, explicit estimand notes), and n=100 seeds is adequate for the main ensembles. However, I found one demonstrably broken estimator: the headline heteroscedasticity indicator bin_variance_slope is dominated by an artifact of flooring |ΔG|=0 (21% of all round trips, verified on the actual C0u/C0p/C2/C3 parquet data) at 1e-9 before taking logs — on real data the floored estimator reads ≈ -0.05 where the zero-excluded version reads +0.99, so the indicator (and the interaction pattern classification built on it) is essentially noise measuring the zero-ΔG fraction. Secondary issues: interaction CIs pair conditions by positional index after dropna(), which silently mispairs seeds when any trial is missing or NaN; the MMFCN-artifact gate verdict is decided from n=2 seeds with no uncertainty; the YH006_1 Hill estimator uses k=10% of the sample (bulk-contaminated) while three other Hill conventions coexist in the repo; and the YH005 acceptance evidence (null tests, 3-model comparison) rests on a single seed with no ACF significance bands. Test coverage of the estimators themselves is regression-only (golden values pinned from the code's own output), so none of these estimator biases would ever trip a test.
area_summaries.spec-drift: I audited documentation/spec vs reality across specs/001-monorepo-consolidation.md, docs/adr/0001, docs/backbone_parity.md, README.md, packages/*/pyproject.toml, imported/*/README.md + CLAUDE.md, CI workflows, tests, and git history. The repo is a consolidation monorepo: packages/ holds canonical cores (abm_models with 8 models incl. SG, stylized_facts, provenance, fingerprint_atlas), experiments/ holds thin core-importing wrappers, imported/ holds 6 subtree-merged research repos, tests/ holds the 74-test workspace suite. Much of the ground truth checks out: both experiments/classical/baseline.py and experiments/speculation_game/baseline.py genuinely import only packages (no imported/ reimplementation); the SG findings-parity numbers in docs/backbone_parity.md match imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/outputs/baseline_metrics.json byte-for-byte and are encoded in tests/test_sg_parity.py; classical bit-parity (test_classical_parity.py) and PRISM-family bit-parity (test_prism_family_parity.py) tests exist and dynamically import the original files; the 5 old GitHub repos really are archived with "[Consolidated→financial-abm-lab]" descriptions; and ADR 0001's "PRISM vs PROV-ABM sg.py byte-identical except import path" claim is exactly true (diff = one import line). The load-bearing drifts are: (1) packages/provenance is a 60-line prov_record shim while the spec's progress summary declares the provenance core "完成" and the target promises PROV-O (PRISM tracker) + L2 ctx (provabm) integration — both remain unextracted in imported/; (2) README.md still says Stage B is 未着手 (not started) although Stage B is merged to main; (3) packages/fingerprint_atlas — a substantial arXiv+LLM pipeline with two auto-committing scheduled CI workflows — appears in no spec, ADR, or README despite specs/ being declared ground truth; (4) AC6's promised `git log --follow` verification returns empty on imported files (history is preserved in the DAG but not via the documented command); (5) stylized_facts is the YH005 analysis promotion only, not the promised PRISM+PROV-ABM unified SF battery. Secondary issues: the AC2 go/no-go parity test is slow-marked and excluded from CI, experiments/ wrappers have zero tests (AC8 partially vacuous), the CI ruff "gate" is neutered by `|| true`, and the fabm scaffold conventions the README promises for Stage B (config-driven runs, make_rngs seed spawning, run tables) are dead code with empty test files. Overall health: the parity/test discipline around the canonical models is genuinely strong; the docs lag reality in both directions (understating completed Stage B work, overstating provenance/SF completeness) and one whole research line is spec-invisible. The capital-allocation shim claim (spec O5, use-shared-provenance branch) could not be verified — the external repo is not accessible from this session.
area_summaries.research-methods-review: I reviewed the research-design documents of the user's active Speculation Game line: imported/speculation-game-info/docs/{findings.md, hypotheses.md, YH006_research_dossier.md, literature.md}, experiments/YH005/YH005_PLAN.md (+README/outputs for verification), YH006/SPEC.md, YH006_1/{SPECv2.0.md, OVERVIEW.md, plans/stage_*.md}, and the three YH008 REPORT files. The program is a well-run sequential replication chain (YH001-YH005: Cont-Bouchaud, Lux-Marchesi, MG, GCMG, SG) culminating in an original contribution: porting the Katahira-Chen Speculation Game onto a PAMS limit-order book (YH006/YH006_1) and testing how matching friction alters the model's wealth-driven 'funnel' structure and agent turnover, plus a parallel LLM-interpretability line (YH008, loss-conditional ATH asymmetry in Llama-3.1-8B). Methodological hygiene is unusually high for a solo project: pre-registered stage plans with stop criteria, plan-approve-run-diff workflow, bit-parity contracts, determinism guards, matched-estimand corrections (raw censoring retired for matched KM survival), and honest post-mortems (P4-P6 lessons on estimand ordering and trial allocation). The dossier is candid about limitations (c_ticks self-consistency, single liquidity ecology, T=1500 vs 50000). The main design-level weaknesses a peer reviewer would press are: (1) the headline funnel and survival claims lack the natural null models (diffusive-P benchmark, random-decision control, event-time/per-trade hazard), so 'LOB friction destroys the mechanism' is not yet separated from 'LOB reduces trade counts and selects which round-trips complete'; (2) the LOB migration changes the cognitive-threshold anchoring (absolute C=3.0, ~1000x sd of Δp, vs 3x median|Δmid|), the agent ecology (+30 MMFCN), and the fill-selection process simultaneously, and only some of these are controlled; (3) the 'interaction is truly null, not underpowered' claim overreads a CI of [-0.042, +0.002]; (4) legacy findings.md entries assert paper-consistency from single seeds without CIs, and Hill-exponent usage lacks any xmin/CI discipline; (5) YH008's Stage-2 gate passed via a post-hoc subgroup confirmed on the same data; and (6) hypotheses.md is stale and unfalsifiable as written. Overall health: strong engineering and increasing statistical maturity, with specific, fixable gaps in null models and attribution rather than sloppy execution.
area_summaries.research-strategy-review: financial-abm-lab is a solo researcher's monorepo consolidating 6 financial-ABM repos (spec 001 is declared ground truth). packages/abm_models holds 8 canonical, parity-verified models behind a REGISTRY + ABMModel protocol; packages/stylized_facts is the "unified" SF battery; packages/fingerprint_atlas is a newer 9-feature stylized-fact geometry (atlas/novelty/inverse) with an LLM loop (Groq proposal generator, arXiv literature DB, preference learning); experiments/ are thin core-importing wrappers for YH001-005; the active research line YH006/YH006_1 (SG on LOB, survival-gap/funnel finding) lives in imported/speculation-game-info with a detailed dossier. Data flow: model.run(seed) -> return series -> stylized facts -> parity tests / atlas fingerprints -> SQLite runs/proposals DB -> PCA atlas + gate.json in notebooks/. Overall engineering health is high (bit-parity tests, RNG contracts, honest failure logs), but research strategy has drifted: every commit since 2026-06-27 builds fingerprint_atlas tooling — a package that appears in no spec and violates spec 001's own out-of-scope clause — while the dossier's explicit next steps (S1-secondary, S6 A3 ablation ensemble, plan A/B decision) have been frozen since ~2026-06-07. The atlas's own validation gate shows silhouette collapsing from 0.195 (v1) to 0.0005 (v4, negative in v3_periods), yet the LLM/preference tower was built on top anyway; the advertised INVERSE experiment (which model is nearest to real SPX/BTC?) is never actually computed despite all ingredients being in the DB. fingerprint_atlas also re-implements Hill/ACF/kurtosis with conventions incompatible with packages/stylized_facts, so atlas numbers are not comparable to the YH findings. Several cheap, self-identified experiments (paper-1 Figs 11-13 from existing round_trips logs, 100-trial Hill α convergence, tie-break sensitivity against the just-committed dissertation Java reference) remain unrun. 2.2 GB of YH006_1 parquet ensembles are committed into git history, and the fabm scaffold (config/rng conventions promised in the README appendix) is dead code with two empty test files.
area_summaries.static-analysis: Static-analysis lane over /home/user/financial-abm-lab: a uv-workspace monorepo whose canonical cores live in packages/ (abm_models with 8 models incl. the SG, stylized_facts, provenance, fingerprint_atlas), thin experiment wrappers in experiments/, the workspace test suite in tests/, and six subtree-merged research repos in imported/ (speculation-game-info = active YH001..YH008 line). Overall static health is good: `uv run python -m compileall -q` over packages/, src/, experiments/ and imported/speculation-game-info/ produces zero SyntaxErrors, and a repo-wide ruff sweep for the dangerous classes (F821 undefined names, F811 redefinitions, E711/E712 comparison bugs) is completely clean — the 31 workspace and 78 speculation-game-info ruff hits are all hygiene-class (F401 unused imports in scripts, F541 empty f-strings, F841 dead locals, E702 semicolons), none in __init__ API re-exports. The real defects are in dependency declarations: packages/abm_models declares only numpy but its package __init__ unconditionally imports cont_bouchaud, which imports scipy at module top level, so a standalone install of the canonical abm-models package cannot even be imported (masked today because the root workspace project declares scipy); and imported/speculation-game-info/requirements.txt (numpy, matplotlib, scipy, powerlaw, pams==0.2.2) omits torch/transformers/transformer_lens/pandas/statsmodels/pyyaml that the active YH008 experiment imports at module top level — and neither pams nor torch is installed in the consolidated env, so the highest-priority research line is not runnable from the monorepo environment as declared. Notebook validation found exactly two notebook-like files: imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH006/notebooks/CI2002.ipynb is valid nbformat-4 JSON with 8 cells, while notebooks/00_sanity_check.jpynb is a zero-byte file with a misspelled extension (.jpynb) that fails JSON parsing; the rest of notebooks/ contains only PNG/JSON output artifacts, and nbformat/jupyter are not installed despite ipykernel being a dev dep. Root pyproject also declares seven dependencies (pandas, polars, statsmodels, pydantic, pyyaml, pyarrow, tqdm) that no code in packages/, src/, experiments/ or tests/ imports, and src/fabm (the README-referenced multi-seed RNG design) is dead: the root project has no build-system, `import fabm` fails, and nothing references it.
area_summaries.smoke-runtime: Empirical smoke-runtime lane over /home/user/financial-abm-lab using the synced .venv (Python 3.11.15). (1) REGISTRY enumerates 8 models: speculation_game, cont_bouchaud, lux_marchesi, minority_game, gcmg, chiarella_iori, zero_intelligence, franke_westerhoff. Every model was run at tiny sizes twice with the same seed (with the GLOBAL random/np.random state deliberately perturbed between runs) and once with a different seed: all 8 are bit-identical under the same seed, all 8 produce different output under a different seed, and reusing the same model instance for two runs is also bit-identical — no global-RNG leakage or hidden mutable state detected anywhere. (2) uv run pytest tests/test_sg_parity.py -v: 2 passed in 38.5s. (3) Protocol conformance: all 8 registry classes satisfy isinstance(x, ABMModel) (runtime_checkable Protocol in packages/abm_models/abm_models/base.py), expose a correct `name` attribute matching their registry key, and implement run(*, seed) keyword-only as declared. (4) SG medium run (N=1000, M=5, S=2, T=2000, seed=7): vectorized backend best-of-2 = 3.55s, reference backend = 6.24s, ratio only 1.76x; ALL 14 top-level result keys plus all 6 arrays inside the nested round_trips dict (418,001 round-trip records) are bit-identical between backends — parity is genuinely exact, including dtypes. Overall runtime health is excellent: the only genuine defects found in this lane are stdout noise from cont_bouchaud's library code (unavoidable print at t=0 for any nonzero report_every, and the ContBouchaud registry wrapper defaults to report_every=5000, unlike LuxMarchesi which forces verbose=False), and the observation that the "vectorized" SG backend delivers a much smaller speedup than the name suggests because bit-parity with the reference RNG-consumption order forces per-agent Python loops (per-close strategy review with individual rng.choice/rng.integers calls) to remain. A minor uniformity wart: lux_marchesi's result dict embeds the Params dataclass object itself, making its result dict non-array-serializable unlike every other model.
area_summaries.yh006-env: Empirical run-lane audit of YH006 (SG-on-LOB via the pams library) and YH005 in imported/speculation-game-info. Environment: pams installs cleanly through the proxy via `uv run --with pams` and resolves to 0.2.2, exactly matching the requirements.txt pin, so there is no version/API drift versus current pams. GREEN: YH006 tests pass 13/13 (parity vs YH005, round-trip invariants, wealth conservation, aggregate parity) with PYTHONPATH=experiments/YH006; YH005 tests pass 18/18; run_experiment.py's core run_condition() executes a C3 (MMFCN+SG) run end-to-end and produces a YH005-compatible result dict; aggregate_sim.py (pure-numpy, pams-free) and all analysis modules import cleanly; run_aggregate_c0.py runs end-to-end standalone and regenerates its metrics/figures. BROKEN-BY-DRIFT: when the FCN liquidity layer was redesigned into a custom MMFCNAgent (configs/_base.py "MM 特化 FCN 仕様 (3B)"), tests and run_experiment.py were updated to runner.class_register(MMFCNAgent), but three sibling entry points were not — smoke_test.py, calibrate_c_ticks.py, and profile_c3_plan_a.py all crash at pams setup with "AttributeError: class for MMFCNAgent is found 0 times" (verified; a one-line registration fix makes smoke_test complete). Knock-on effect: the SPEC-mandated pipeline step 1 (C_ticks calibration) is unrunnable and outputs/C_ticks_calibration.json is absent, so run_experiment.py silently falls back to c_ticks=0.03 while the actual SPEC §3.2(e) procedure (re-executed with the script's own defaults) yields ~9.02 and the profiler hardcodes 28.006 — a ~300x quantizer-scale error for anyone reproducing the experiment today. Secondary hazards: run_aggregate_c0.py resolves its `from analysis import ...` to YH005/analysis.py only by an accidental import-order side effect (YH006's own empty analysis/ package shadows it under any other import order), and it has no argument parsing, so `--help` executes the full experiment and overwrites tracked result PNGs in place. Overall health: the tested core is solid and reproducible; the untested standalone scripts around it have bitrotted.

findings_verified.title: bin_variance_slope is dominated by the 1e-9 log floor on 21% exact-zero integer dG; sign of the headline heteroscedasticity indicator flips when zeros are handled
findings_verified.detail: dG (cognitive P&L) is int64 (YH005/simulate.py:82,234: rt_delta_G: list[int], np.asarray(..., dtype=np.int64)), and in the researcher's own stored data (YH006_1/data/phase1_reanalysis_round_trips.parquet, 2,093,574 RTs) 21.1% of round trips have dG == 0, with the zero-rate horizon-dependent (23.8% for h<=median vs 17.8% for h>median) and condition-dependent (C0u 0.212, C0p 0.211, C3 0.151, C2 0.116). bin_variance_slope maps each zero to log(1e-9) = -20.7 while nonzero integer |dG| gives log|dG| >= 0, so Var(log|dG|) per bin is dominated by the zero-fraction term f(1-f)*(~22)^2 ~ 77, swamping the genuine dispersion (order 1). The indicator therefore measures the horizon-profile of zero-dG frequency, not the funnel. Re-running on the stored parquet: C0u as-implemented -0.203 vs zeros-excluded +0.995; C0p -0.231 vs +0.978; C2 -0.176 vs +0.685; C3 -0.173 vs +0.736. Floor sensitivity on C0u: floor=1e-9 -> -0.203, floor=0.5 -> +0.978 (pure artifact of the constant). The negative pooled bin_var_slope values (-0.40 C0u, -0.29 C0p, -0.13 C3) are quoted throughout YH006_1/README.md (lines 68-69, 106, 173, 189-245) and drive the 'microstructure 真効果' conclusion, the C2_A1/C3_A1 causal-candidate reasoning, and the S5.5 gate; the cross-condition ranking closely tracks the zero-dG fractions, suggesting those comparisons are partly ranking conditions by zero frequency. This is one of the 5 main indicators (SPEC 4.1/4.2) and feeds bin_variance_slope_pooled, aggregate_full_summary, aggregate_ensemble, ablation summaries and bootstrap CIs.

bin_vars.append(float(np.var(np.log(np.maximum(np.abs(dG[mask]), 1e-9)))))  # analysis.py:75

findings_verified.suggestion: Decide explicitly how zero-dG round trips enter the funnel metric: either exclude dG==0 before Var(log|dG|) and report the zero fraction per bin as a separate indicator, or use a variance measure defined for zeros (e.g. Var of asinh(dG), or IQR of dG per bin). Then re-run S1-S6 aggregation and re-derive the README conclusions; also add a floor-sensitivity check to the pipeline.
findings_verified.verify_note: I attempted to refute and failed on every point; the finding reproduces exactly. (1) Code: imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH006_1/code/analysis.py:75 does `np.var(np.log(np.maximum(np.abs(dG[mask]), 1e-9)))`, and bin_variance_slope_pooled (lines 82-97) is a thin wrapper over the same function, so the pooled headline indicator inherits the floor. (2) Data: re-ran on the stored phase1_reanalysis_round_trips.parquet (2,093,574 RTs) with the project venv: frac(delta_g==0) = 0.2114 overall (C0u 0.2117, C0p 0.2113, C3 0.1509, C2 0.1160), zero rate 0.238 for h<=median vs 0.178 for h>median — all matching the reported numbers. (3) Sign flip: importing the actual bin_variance_slope from analysis.py gives C0u -0.2033, C0p -0.2308, C2 -0.1758, C3 -0.1727 as implemented, versus +0.9945, +0.9780, +0.6848, +0.7364 with zeros excluded; changing only the floor constant from 1e-9 to 0.5 flips C0u from -0.2033 to +0.9780, proving the negative sign is an artifact of the arbitrary constant, not the data. The mechanism is mathematically sound: each dG==0 contributes log(1e-9) = -20.7 while every nonzero integer |dG| contributes log|dG| >= 0, so per-bin Var(log|dG|) is dominated by the Bernoulli zero-fraction term f(1-f)*(~22)^2, and the horizon-decreasing zero rate forces a spurious negative slope. (4) No mitigation: grepped YH006_1 code, plans, and README for any zero-dG exclusion or documented intent — none exists; delta_g comes from int64 rt_delta_G in YH005 simulate.py, so exact zeros are structural. (5) Impact: README.md quotes the artifact values as headline results (lines 68-69: pooled bin_var_slope C0u -0.4036, C0p -0.2879; also lines 23, 56, 106, 134, 189-207) and the plans (stage_S4_plan.md line 30) explicitly base the S5 design on the pooled bin_var interaction (-0.258); the S5.5 L2 gate uses aggregate_ablation_summary.py's ci_excludes_zero test on this indicator. Cross-condition slope ordering tracks the zero-dG fractions, so condition comparisons are partly ranking zero frequency. Severity: this is the user's own active research line (YH006 in YH001..YH008), and a headline indicator of one of the 5 main SPEC 4.1/4.2 metrics reports the wrong sign and drives stated conclusions — critical is correctly calibrated.

findings_verified.title: Reconcile misclassifies mid-step fills: all 100 SG agents permanently desync from their real LOB inventory (runaway positions, corrupted round trips and wealth)
findings_verified.detail: Verified as reported, with two refinements. (a) The misclassifying condition is 'else' after 'abs(actual_vol) < entry_quantity', i.e. abs(actual_vol) >= entry_quantity — the in-code comment claims '== entry_quantity' but the branch also swallows over-magnitude states (|actual| > q), which my probe shows dominate once runaway starts (4425/4857 else-hits at seed 42, main=500). (b) The sign-flip is the majority case, not a corner: 4538/4857 (93%) of 'close cancelled' classifications occurred while the agent's real inventory had the opposite sign of its believed position. The partial-close branch (lines 372-376) coincidentally resyncs belief to reality when abs(actual) < q (only 4 sign-flipped partials observed), so the unrepaired divergence flows entirely through the else branch; the line-222 stale-flatten guard requires position == 0 and never fires for these agents. Reproduced the original probe bit-for-bit (seed 777: 100/100 agents desynced, first desync t=201 median, final gap median 495 / max 15529 shares, mean real inventory -536.7 while agents believe near-flat). No test in YH006/tests or the workspace suite checks asset_volumes against position*entry_quantity, so nothing guards this. All C2/C3 LOB-condition results in the YH006 README are computed on this corrupted execution layer.
findings_verified.evidence: speculation_agent.py:372-381: 'elif abs(actual_vol) < self.entry_quantity: self.position = 1 if actual_vol > 0 else -1; self.entry_quantity = abs(actual_vol) ... else: # actual_vol == self.entry_quantity: close MARKET_ORDER が前 step では約定せず... self.close_cancelled += 1' — no sign check, so actual=-q after an unintended fill+resell is read as 'no fill'. Guard at :222 'if self.pending_intent is None and self.position == 0:' only repairs flat agents. Probe output: 'seed=777: agents_desynced=100/100; first desync t: min=201 median=201 max=573; final |actual-believed| gap: median=495 max=15529; final actual inventory: min=-15529 max=8757 mean=-536.7'.
findings_verified.suggestion: Make reality the source of truth every step: when pending_intent is None, assert/repair asset_volumes == position*entry_quantity (extend the stale-flatten path to position != 0 by flattening the excess |asset - position*q|); in the close branch, classify by signed actual_vol relative to the pre-close position, not |actual_vol|; add a per-step desync counter to _meta so any recurrence is visible; re-run C2/C3 and re-derive the 2x2 tables after the fix.
findings_verified.verify_note: Confirmed by code reading of both sides of the interaction plus two independent empirical reproductions. (1) Mechanism: pams 0.2.2 SequentialRunner._collect_orders_from_normal_agents (sequential.py:394-425) calls every agent's submit_orders first — so each SpeculationAgent's _reconcile snapshot of asset_volumes is taken during collection — and _handle_orders (sequential.py:427-471) then processes the shuffled order lists sequentially with market._execution() after each. A leftover close MARKET_ORDER from the previous step remains live in the book (market orders match at top priority) and can fill after the agent's snapshot and before its Cancel is processed. (2) Defect: _reconcile's close else branch (speculation_agent.py:377-381) fires for abs(actual_vol) >= entry_quantity with NO sign check and increments close_cancelled as if nothing filled; the comment even claims '==' while the condition is '>='. The stale-fill recovery (line 222) requires pending_intent is None AND position == 0, so it never repairs an agent that believes it holds a position. The partial branch (lines 372-376) happens to resync belief when abs(actual)<entry_quantity, but the >=-magnitude/sign-flipped states loop forever: the agent resubmits close of q each step while real inventory diverges. (3) Empirical: re-ran the prior probe unchanged in the sandbox venv (C3 config, warmup=200/main=800, num_sg=100, c_ticks=28.0, seed 777): output identical to the finding — 100/100 agents desynced, first desync min/median t=201, max t=573, final |actual-believed| gap median=495 max=15529, mean final real inventory -536.7 shares vs near-flat beliefs. (4) Wrote an independent targeted probe patching _reconcile (seed 42, main=500): the close else branch fired 4857 times, of which 4538 (93%) had sign-flipped real inventory and 4425 had |actual| > entry_quantity — i.e., the 'close cancelled' classification is predominantly wrong in practice, not an edge case. (5) No mitigation: YH006/tests (test_roundtrip_invariants.py, test_wealth_conservation.py, etc.) contain no check comparing asset_volumes to position*entry_quantity — grep for asset_volumes/inventory in tests returns nothing; the workspace suite cannot catch this. Severity 'critical' is calibrated correctly: YH006 is part of the user's own active research line (speculation-game-info, YH001..YH008), and every C2/C3 README statistic and the headline 2x2 LOB-interaction conclusion is computed on top of this desynced execution layer (round trips, sg_wealth, bankruptcy, and the mid-price all corrupted by the runaway one-sided flow).

findings_verified.title: Workspace bit-parity guard is drastically weaker than the YH005 parity suite it supersedes; the richer suite is not collected
findings_verified.detail: The canonical-package parity guard tests/test_sg_parity.py:63 (test_sg_reference_vectorized_bit_parity) checks one seed and only 4 of the ~12 output surfaces (prices, cognitive_prices, num_substitutions, total_wealth). The far richer YH005 suite (imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/tests/test_parity.py: 5 seeds, 8 parity keys, 6 round_trips arrays, 3 order-size arrays, per-agent final_wealth, S=1, Null A history_mode='exogenous', Null B decision_mode='random', action-count and cumsum invariants) is excluded from collection by pyproject.toml:41 testpaths=["tests","packages"], and CI additionally skips the slow statistical parity test (-m "not slow"), leaving the weak test as the sole CI gate for the canonical port. Moreover — worse than the original finding states — even if collected, the YH005 suite would not guard the canonical package: it sys.path-imports the experiment-local model.py/simulate.py (test_parity.py:26-29), so its assertions cover the imported copy, not packages/abm_models/abm_models/sg. The canonical package fully implements the unasserted surfaces (round_trips, num_orders_by_size buckets, per-agent final_wealth, both Null modes), so a future edit to packages/abm_models/abm_models/sg/vectorized.py that drifts in Null-mode RNG loops, round-trip logging, order-size buckets, or per-agent wealth would pass workspace CI while silently breaking the bit-parity the ADR/backbone_parity docs claim. Fix: port the YH005 parity assertions into tests/test_sg_parity.py targeting abm_models.sg (all output keys, multiple seeds, S=1, Null A/B, invariants).
