(1) imported/speculation-game-info/README.md contains no reference to packages/, canonical status, or consolidation — it presents YH001-005 as the live implementations. No imported/README.md exists; canonical-pointer signage lives only in root README and docs/adr/0001. (2) imported/ABM-Microstructure/CLAUDE.md still instructs an active 'spec → plan → tasks → implement (/speckit-*)' workflow, and its speckit skills are indeed loaded into the monorepo skill list scoped to that directory. (3) Duplication is real: imported YH001 model.py is byte-identical to packages/abm_models/abm_models/cont_bouchaud/model.py, and YH005 model.py differs from packages/.../sg/reference.py only in one relative-import line. (4) Mitigation is exactly as described: tests/test_classical_parity.py dynamically imports from imported/speculation-game-info/experiments, but the SG imported-vs-canonical guard (tests/test_sg_parity.py::test_sg_findings_parity_within_5pct) is @pytest.mark.slow and excluded by default per pyproject.toml; the non-slow SG test only checks packages-internal reference-vs-vectorized parity. Since YH006-008 development intentionally continues inside imported/speculation-game-info, the risk of an agent editing the duplicated YH005 copy under stale local ground truth is concrete. Severity minor is correctly calibrated: documentation/architecture hygiene with partial test mitigation, not a correctness bug.

findings_verified.title: fingerprint_atlas re-implements Hill/ACF/kurtosis with conventions incompatible with the 'unified' stylized_facts package — atlas α values are not comparable to YH findings
findings_verified.detail: packages/fingerprint_atlas re-implements Hill/ACF/kurtosis with conventions incompatible with packages/stylized_facts (the spec-001 "unified" SF battery) and never imports it: fingerprint.py uses tail_frac=5% plus a HILL_ALPHA_CAP=20.0 (lines 54/57/78) vs stylized_facts' k=sqrt(n) (core.py:86). Because adapters.py/populate.py fingerprint the canonical SG ("speculation_game" from abm_models.REGISTRY) and store "hill_tail_index" in the atlas runs DB, the atlas α and the YH/backbone-parity "Hill α = 4.52669" (docs/backbone_parity.md:23) are different statistics under the same name — a latent apples-to-oranges hazard if ever cross-cited. Two ACF implementations now coexist inside packages/ (stylized_facts._acf, fingerprint._autocorr) despite spec 001's consolidation goal (the third copy in imported/YH005/analysis.py is the frozen pre-extraction original, expected there). Mitigation lowering severity: the fingerprint conventions are deliberate and documented — the cap and fixed tail fraction serve inverse-variance-weighted distance geometry per the Gao et al. stylized-facts-distance literature cited in the module docstring, hill_tail_index_raw is provided as the honest diagnostic, and both pipelines are internally consistent with passing tests. No computation is currently wrong; the defect is naming/architecture drift whose bite requires a future cross-citation. Recommended fix: rename the atlas feature (e.g. hill_alpha_5pct_capped) or route it through stylized_facts.hill_mle_tail_index(k=...) so the convention is explicit.
findings_verified.evidence: fingerprint.py:54 'HILL_ALPHA_CAP = 20.0', :57/:78 'tail_frac: float = 0.05'; stylized_facts/core.py:86 'k = int(np.sqrt(sorted_desc.size))'; rg 'stylized_facts' packages/fingerprint_atlas → no import hits.
findings_verified.suggestion: Make fingerprint.py consume packages/stylized_facts primitives, exposing tail_frac/k and the cap as explicit parameters, and add a short docs note stating which Hill convention each recorded number uses. At minimum rename the atlas column hill_alpha_5pct_cap20 so the two statistics can never be conflated.
findings_verified.verify_note: Every factual claim verifies: fingerprint.py:54 caps Hill at 20.0 and uses tail_frac=0.05 (lines 57/78), stylized_facts/core.py:86 defaults k=sqrt(n) (~141 vs ~1000 tail points at T=20000 — materially different α given Hill's k-sensitivity, which YH002 itself documented at 1.92/2.40/2.59 across tail fractions). The only 'stylized_facts' strings inside fingerprint_atlas are the unrelated DB column stylized_facts_targeted (paper metadata), so zero package reuse is correct. The collision is live: adapters.py registers "speculation_game" from abm_models.REGISTRY and populate.py:73-78 writes fingerprint() (capped 5%-tail Hill) into the runs DB for the canonical SG, while docs/backbone_parity.md:23 records Hill α=4.52669 for the same SG baseline via stylized_facts (sqrt(n)) — same label, different statistic. The ACF triplication is real, though the third copy sits in imported/ (frozen original, expected under spec 001's history-preservation constraint); the genuine post-consolidation duplication is the two copies inside packages/ (stylized_facts._acf vs fingerprint._autocorr), contradicting spec 001's "SF battery 統一" intent. Severity correction: fingerprint.py's conventions are deliberate and documented (cap rationale lines 51-53/79-84; 5% tail follows the Gao et al. stylized-facts-distance literature it cites) because the value is a coordinate in a distance geometry, not a publishable tail exponent; neither pipeline computes wrong numbers by its own definition and all internal parity/atlas tests pass. The harm requires a future human cross-citation under the shared name — a genuine latent hazard (the atlas even drafts paper text via methods_draft.py) but not a defect currently biting, so minor rather than major.

findings_verified.title: 2520 parquet files (~2.2 GB, git pack 1.74 GiB) of ensemble outputs committed to history — contradicts the repo's own results-table convention and will burden every future clone
findings_verified.detail: git ls-files shows 2520 .parquet + 32 .log files under imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH006_1; du reports 2.2 GB of the repo's 2.3 GB imported/ footprint, and git count-objects shows a 1.74 GiB pack — the subtree merge permanently baked raw ensemble outputs into the monorepo history that spec 001 intends as the permanent home. The README appendix's own convention says results should be saved as tables keyed by run_id/git_commit/config/seed (i.e., regenerable artifacts, compact summaries in git). Since the ensembles are deterministic given seed 1000-1099 and the parity-tested code, the raw per-trial parquets are re-derivable; what is NOT captured anywhere machine-readable is the summary layer (the dossier's tables are markdown). This matters strategically because the plan is to archive old repos and treat this monorepo as canonical — meaning this weight is now permanent unless history is rewritten before wider adoption, which gets more expensive every week.
findings_verified.evidence: git ls-files imported/.../YH006_1 | ext count: 2520 parquet; du -sh → 2.2G; git count-objects -vH → size-pack: 1.74 GiB; README appendix: '結果は graph だけでなく table で保存: run_id, timestamp, git_commit, ...'.
findings_verified.suggestion: Decide now: either accept the weight explicitly (document it) or filter-repo the parquets out while only two branches exist, moving raw ensembles to external storage (OneDrive already in the workflow) with a manifest (seed, config hash, sha256) committed in their place. Going forward, commit summary tables only.
findings_verified.verify_note: Every factual claim reproduces exactly: `git ls-files imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH006_1` shows 2520 .parquet and 32 .log tracked files; `du -sh` on the directory reports 2.2G; `git count-objects -vH` reports size-pack 1.74 GiB, so the blobs are permanently in the object store via the subtree merge (commit 29c649a "Add 'imported/speculation-game-info/' ..."). The cited convention exists verbatim at /home/user/financial-abm-lab/README.md line 57 ("結果は graph だけでなく **table で保存**: run_id, timestamp, git_commit, config_path, seed, ..."). The repo's own root .gitignore even lists `*.parquet` and `*.csv`, confirming the monorepo's intent NOT to track such artifacts — but gitignore cannot un-track files already committed through the subtree merge, so the defect is not mitigated. Nothing guards or removes this weight; only a history rewrite (filter-repo/LFS migration) before the monorepo becomes canonical would. Severity 'minor' is correctly calibrated: it is a genuine hygiene/repo-weight issue with no effect on result correctness, though with a strategic time-pressure component (cost of history rewrite grows as the monorepo accrues commits and clones).

findings_verified.title: Canonical abm-models package cannot be imported standalone: top-level scipy import but pyproject declares only numpy
findings_verified.detail: packages/abm_models/pyproject.toml declares only numpy, but abm_models/cont_bouchaud/model.py:10-11 imports scipy.sparse at module top level and abm_models/__init__.py:17 imports ContBouchaud unconditionally, so a standalone install of abm-models per its own metadata cannot even be imported (verified: fresh venv + `uv pip install packages/abm_models` -> `import abm_models` raises ModuleNotFoundError: scipy). gcmg/model.py:282 has an additional lazy scipy dependency (binomial_theory). Masked in the monorepo because the root project declares scipy. Fails loudly, never silently, and no external consumer currently exists, so impact is low; fix is adding "scipy" to the package's dependencies.
findings_verified.evidence:
packages/abm_models/pyproject.toml: dependencies = ["numpy"]
packages/abm_models/abm_models/cont_bouchaud/model.py:10-11:
  from scipy.sparse import csr_matrix
  from scipy.sparse.csgraph import connected_components
packages/abm_models/abm_models/__init__.py:17: from .cont_bouchaud import ContBouchaud
Verified: `uv pip list` shows abm-models installed editable with scipy provided only by the root project.
findings_verified.suggestion: Add "scipy" to packages/abm_models/pyproject.toml dependencies (or make the cont_bouchaud scipy import lazy and declare scipy as an extra, mirroring stylized_facts' viz extra pattern). Add a CI check that each workspace package imports cleanly in an isolated env with only its declared deps.
findings_verified.verify_note: Empirically reproduced. Facts check out: (1) /home/user/financial-abm-lab/packages/abm_models/pyproject.toml line 6 declares dependencies = ["numpy"] only; (2) cont_bouchaud/model.py lines 10-11 import scipy.sparse at module top level; (3) abm_models/__init__.py line 17 unconditionally imports ContBouchaud, so `import abm_models` triggers the scipy import; (4) gcmg/model.py line 282 also imports scipy.stats.binom (lazily, inside binomial_theory). I built a fresh venv and ran `uv pip install ./packages/abm_models` followed by `import abm_models`: it raises ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scipy' exactly as claimed (traceback through cont_bouchaud/__init__.py -> model.py:10). The defect is masked in the monorepo only because the root pyproject.toml declares scipy. No mitigation exists elsewhere (no try/except, no optional-dependency extra, no test of standalone installability). However, severity is miscalibrated: this does not produce wrong results or data corruption, and it fails loudly and immediately at import time with an obvious error, only in environments outside the monorepo (which currently has no external consumer). It is a genuine metadata bug with a one-line fix (add "scipy" to dependencies), i.e. genuine but low-impact -> minor.

findings_verified.title: Active research line YH008 not reproducible from declared manifest: torch/transformers/transformer_lens/pandas/statsmodels/yaml all undeclared
findings_verified.detail: imported/speculation-game-info/requirements.txt (referenced by that repo's README setup instructions) is stale: it omits torch/transformers/transformer_lens/yaml needed by YH008 (top-level `import torch` at experiments/YH008/src/model.py:23 and freeze_groups.py:13) and pandas/statsmodels used throughout YH006_1, and the README falsely states pandas is not yet imported. A fresh standalone install per the README fails with ImportError for YH006_1 analysis and all of YH008. Impact is limited: specs/001-monorepo-consolidation.md documents an explicit user decision that YH006/007/008 remain in imported/ outside the consolidated workspace (YH008 is a GPU/LLM lineage requiring Llama-3.1-8B weights regardless), so absence of torch/pams from the monorepo env and exclusion of their tests from workspace testpaths is by design; pandas/statsmodels/pyyaml are already declared and installed via the root pyproject.toml. Remaining fix: update the imported repo's requirements.txt (or add per-experiment manifests for YH006_1/YH008) and correct the README dependency notes.
findings_verified.evidence:
imported/speculation-game-info/requirements.txt (complete):
  numpy
  matplotlib
  scipy
  powerlaw
  pams==0.2.2
imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH008/src/model.py:23: import torch
imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH006/run_experiment.py:22: from pams.runners import SequentialRunner
`uv pip list | grep -iE 'pams|torch'` → empty.
findings_verified.suggestion: Either extend requirements.txt (torch, transformers, transformer_lens, pandas, statsmodels, pyyaml) with pinned versions, or add per-experiment requirements files (YH006: pams==0.2.2; YH008: torch/transformers/transformer_lens), and consider an optional-dependency group in the root pyproject (e.g. [project.optional-dependencies] yh = [...]) so the active line runs under uv.
findings_verified.verify_note: Facts verified: requirements.txt lists only numpy/matplotlib/scipy/powerlaw/pams, while YH008 imports torch at top level (src/model.py:23, freeze_groups.py:13) plus transformers/transformer_lens (model.py:56-57) and yaml, and YH006_1 imports pandas/statsmodels in ~20 scripts; the repo README's setup section points at this requirements.txt and even claims pandas is "not yet imported" (stale). uv pip list confirms torch/pams/transformers absent from the workspace env, while pandas/statsmodels/pyyaml are present via root pyproject.toml. However, the auditor's severity framing overstates impact: specs/001-monorepo-consolidation.md explicitly records a user scope decision that YH006/007/008 stay in imported/speculation-game-info, excluded from core migration ("LOB/LLM は別系譜、core 移行対象外"; YH008 = "LLM/GPU 別系譜"), so their non-executability from the consolidated env and exclusion from workspace testpaths is documented design, not silent breakage. YH008 additionally requires GPU + Llama-3.1-8B weights, which no manifest entry would fix. pandas/statsmodels/yaml gaps are covered by the root workspace manifest. What survives is a genuine but low-impact defect: the imported repo's own declared manifest/README cannot reproduce YH006_1 or YH008 in a standalone install, producing an ImportError rather than wrong results.

findings_verified.title: Root project declares 7 dependencies no workspace code imports, masking the real dependency surface of the packages
findings_verified.detail: Root dependencies include pandas, polars, statsmodels, pydantic, pyyaml, pyarrow and tqdm, yet an AST scan plus grep over all of packages/, src/, experiments/ and tests/ finds zero imports of any of them (third-party imports actually used: numpy, scipy, matplotlib, groq, arxiv, pytest). This bloat is not just cosmetic: the over-broad root env is precisely what hides the abm-models missing-scipy defect and the fingerprint-atlas missing-matplotlib declaration (analytics.py/atlas.py lazily import matplotlib in 10 places but the package declares numpy/abm-models/stylized-facts/groq/arxiv only — no viz extra, unlike stylized_facts which correctly has one). Every package 'works' in the workspace regardless of what it declares, so declaration drift is never caught.
findings_verified.evidence:
pyproject.toml dependencies = ["numpy", "pandas", "polars", "scipy", "statsmodels", "matplotlib", "pydantic", "pyyaml", "pyarrow", "tqdm", ...]
grep -rn 'import polars|import pyarrow|import tqdm|import pydantic|import yaml|import pandas|import statsmodels' packages/ src/ experiments/ tests/ → no matches
packages/fingerprint_atlas/pyproject.toml: dependencies = ["numpy", "abm-models", "stylized-facts", "groq", "arxiv"] (no matplotlib) vs fingerprint_atlas/analytics.py:80 'import matplotlib.pyplot as plt'
findings_verified.suggestion: Trim root deps to what workspace code imports (numpy, scipy, matplotlib + the four workspace packages), add matplotlib as a viz extra to fingerprint-atlas mirroring stylized-facts, and gate declaration drift with a tool like deptry or an isolated-install import smoke test per package.
findings_verified.verify_note: All factual claims check out. (1) Root pyproject.toml lines 5-20 declare pandas, polars, statsmodels, pydantic, pyyaml, pyarrow, tqdm; a grep for import/from of any of these over packages/, src/, experiments/, tests/, scripts/ returns zero matches (only .venv and imported/ legacy repos use pandas/yaml/tqdm — e.g. imported/market-dynamics/tests/*, which are not part of the workspace suite). (2) The masking mechanism is real: packages/abm_models/pyproject.toml declares dependencies = ["numpy"] only, yet abm_models/cont_bouchaud/model.py imports scipy at module top level (lines 10-11) and gcmg/model.py uses scipy.stats — abm-models installed standalone would break, but never in the fat root env. (3) packages/fingerprint_atlas/pyproject.toml declares ["numpy", "abm-models", "stylized-facts", "groq", "arxiv"] with no matplotlib and no viz extra, while atlas.py (130, 183), label_cli.py (131) and analytics.py (46, 78, ...) lazily import matplotlib. One mitigating nuance: the imported/ subtree repos do use pandas etc., so some root deps may be intentional convenience for running legacy code — but imported/ is not covered by the workspace declarations either, so the drift-masking argument stands. Severity minor (hygiene) is correctly calibrated: no wrong results, only latent packaging breakage if packages are installed outside the workspace.

findings_verified.title: ContBouchaud library code prints to stdout on every run; t=0 always triggers regardless of report_every
findings_verified.detail: simulate() prints progress with `if report_every and t % report_every == 0`. Since 0 % anything == 0, the t=0 line prints for ANY nonzero report_every — verified empirically: with report_every=10**9 the run still emits 'step 0/500'. The only silencing value is report_every=0 (falsy), which is undocumented. Worse, the registry wrapper ContBouchaud (cont_bouchaud/__init__.py:24) defaults report_every=5000 and exposes no verbose flag, so REGISTRY-driven batch sweeps (the intended use per spec 001 O2) pollute stdout with 10 lines per default run. This is inconsistent with the LuxMarchesi wrapper, which hard-codes verbose=False in its run() for exactly this reason.
findings_verified.suggestion: Have the ContBouchaud wrapper pass report_every=0 (mirroring LuxMarchesi's verbose=False), or change the condition to `t > 0 and t % report_every == 0` and route through logging instead of print.
findings_verified.verify_note: The code behaves exactly as claimed. packages/abm_models/abm_models/cont_bouchaud/model.py:77-78 reads `if report_every and t % report_every == 0: print(f"  step {t}/{T}")` inside `for t in range(T)`. Since 0 % k == 0 for any k > 0, the t=0 line prints for every nonzero report_every; the only silencing value is report_every=0 (falsy), which is undocumented in the docstring. The registry wrapper (cont_bouchaud/__init__.py:24) defaults report_every=5000 with no verbose flag, so a default T=50000 run emits 10 stdout lines. This is indeed inconsistent with lux_marchesi/__init__.py:32, which hard-codes verbose=False in its wrapper's run(). No mitigation found elsewhere (no logging config, no wrapper suppression, no capsys guard in the package). It is a stdout-pollution hygiene issue with no effect on numerical results, so severity 'minor' is correctly calibrated.

findings_verified.title: SG 'vectorized' backend is only ~1.76x faster than the per-agent reference at baseline-scale params
findings_verified.detail: Confirmed with independent measurement (T=1000: vectorized 1.86s vs reference 3.16s, ratio 1.70x; parity exact; 209,058 round trips). Note the per-agent loop is an explicitly documented design tradeoff (module docstring, vectorized.py:10-11) to preserve §7 RNG consumption order, so this is a known limitation rather than an oversight. A future fix could batch tie-break draws or relax bit-parity behind a flag for sweep workloads (YH008).
findings_verified.suggestion: Either document the modest speedup honestly (rename to 'batched' or note the parity constraint caps gains), or add a third non-parity 'fast' backend that pre-draws review/bankruptcy randomness in blocks; per-call Generator overhead (rng.choice on a tiny array) dominates and can be amortized with a buffered uniform stream while keeping statistical equivalence.
findings_verified.verify_note: Reproduced the benchmark independently in the project venv (.venv): at N=1000, M=5, S=2, T=1000, seed=7, vectorized.simulate took 1.86s vs run_reference 3.16s — ratio 1.70x, matching the reported ~1.76x. Bit-parity of prices confirmed (np.array_equal True), and round-trip count was 209,058 for 1000 steps, matching the reported ~418k over 2000 steps (~209 closes/step). The cited code is exactly as claimed: packages/abm_models/abm_models/sg/vectorized.py lines 181-207 iterate close_idx in a Python for-loop with per-agent rng.choice/rng.integers calls, and lines 93-99 do a per-agent RNG loop for decision_mode='random'. Both loops are deliberately retained for RNG-consumption-order parity — the module docstring (lines 10-11) explicitly documents this. So the finding is factually accurate: the 'vectorized' backend delivers <2x speedup at baseline scale because step 8 dominates. No caller or test mitigates the performance cost; it is an inherent consequence of the documented bit-parity design constraint, not a correctness bug. Severity 'minor' is correctly calibrated: results are correct, only sweep wall-clock time is affected, and the behavior is intentional/documented.

findings_verified.title: run_aggregate_c0.py has no argument parsing: `python run_aggregate_c0.py --help` silently runs the full experiment and overwrites tracked result PNGs in-place
findings_verified.detail: main() takes no CLI arguments and ignores argv entirely, so any invocation — including `--help` — immediately executes both C0 aggregate conditions and unconditionally overwrites the ten tracked results_c0{u,p}_*.png files in the experiment directory plus outputs/*.json and *.pkl. This bit me during the audit: a `--help` probe modified 10 committed figure files (restored via git checkout afterwards; final `git status --porcelain` is clean and the created outputs/ dir was removed). For an active research line whose committed figures are the record of past runs, a help-flag probe or stray double-run destroying them with no prompt is a real footgun; there is also no seed/steps override even though run_experiment.py next to it has a full argparse CLI.
findings_verified.evidence:
def main() -> None:
    ...  # no argparse; body immediately runs simulate + plots
# observed: `python run_aggregate_c0.py --help` ran the full pipeline and left
#  M results_c0p_*.png / results_c0u_*.png (10 tracked files modified)
findings_verified.suggestion: Add argparse (even a bare parser gives --help for free) and write figures to outputs/ (already gitignored) or a timestamped directory instead of overwriting the committed results_*.png.
findings_verified.verify_note: The claim matches the code exactly. In /home/user/financial-abm-lab/imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH006/run_aggregate_c0.py, main() (lines 163-208) takes no arguments and never inspects sys.argv; the `if __name__ == "__main__": main()` guard (lines 211-212) means any invocation, including `--help`, immediately runs both simulations. figs_dir is set to HERE (line 166), i.e. the experiment directory itself, and _collect_metrics (lines 100-127) writes results_{label}_*.png for label in {c0u, c0p} — exactly the 10 files confirmed tracked by `git ls-files` — with no existence check or prompt, plus outputs/{label}_metrics.json and {label}_result.pkl. The sibling run_experiment.py does have an argparse CLI, so the inconsistency claim also holds. Mitigations are partial only: the seed is fixed (777) so results are deterministic in principle, but PNG bytes depend on matplotlib/environment, and the auditor empirically observed 10 tracked files modified. Current worktree is clean (restored). Severity 'minor' is correctly calibrated: it is a hygiene footgun in an active research line that can clobber committed figures, but it does not produce wrong scientific results and files are recoverable from git.

findings_verified.title: requirements.txt omits pytest (and everything is unpinned except pams), while the parity test suites are the reproducibility ground truth
findings_verified.detail: requirements.txt lists numpy, matplotlib, scipy, powerlaw, pams==0.2.2. The YH005 and YH006 test suites — the mechanism that proves YH006 reproduces YH005 (test_parity.py in both) — import pytest directly (YH005/tests/test_parity.py:24, YH006/tests/test_aggregate_parity.py:21), which is not declared, so `pip install -r requirements.txt && pytest` fails on a clean environment. numpy/scipy/matplotlib are unpinned, so statistical outputs and figures are not environment-reproducible by the file that claims to define the environment. Empirically the suites do run today: with pams 0.2.2 (which uv resolves for the unpinned name, matching the pin exactly — no API drift), YH006 passes 13/13 and YH005 passes 18/18 under PYTHONPATH set to the respective experiment dir.
findings_verified.evidence:
numpy
matplotlib
scipy
powerlaw
pams==0.2.2
# but: imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/tests/test_parity.py:24: import pytest
findings_verified.suggestion: Add pytest to requirements.txt (or a requirements-dev.txt) and pin at least numpy, since the parity thresholds in the tests are the project's reproducibility contract.
findings_verified.verify_note: Verified: /home/user/financial-abm-lab/imported/speculation-game-info/requirements.txt contains only numpy, matplotlib, scipy, powerlaw, pams==0.2.2 — no pytest, and only pams is pinned. The repo's README.md (line 59) instructs `pip install -r requirements.txt` as the setup path, yet the parity suites import pytest directly (experiments/YH005/tests/test_parity.py:24 unconditionally; YH006/tests/test_aggregate_parity.py:21 inside a function). So on a clean env following the imported repo's own instructions, running the parity tests fails with ModuleNotFoundError for pytest. Partial mitigation exists at the monorepo level — root pyproject.toml declares pytest in the [project.optional-dependencies] dev group and the workspace suite passes — but that does not cover a standalone checkout/use of the imported repo, which is the environment its requirements.txt claims to define. The unpinned numpy/scipy/matplotlib reproducibility point is real but soft (statistical outputs may drift across versions; the auditor confirmed current versions pass 18/18 and 13/13). Severity 'minor' is correctly calibrated: no wrong results are produced, it is an environment/setup gap in an imported (though active) research line, easily fixed and mitigated in the monorepo context.

findings_verified.title: Provenance code_version records the git HEAD of the process CWD, not of the PRISM package, and swallows failures to 'unknown'
findings_verified.detail: get_git_commit() (tracker.py:46-58) omits cwd=, so ProvenanceRecord.code_version reflects the caller's CWD repo (or "unknown" on nonzero returncode / outside a repo), with no dirty-tree detection — accurate as reported, but the defect lives only in the retired imported/PRISM lineage; the canonical packages/provenance does not use git version capture, so no active research code is affected.
findings_verified.evidence: result = subprocess.run(["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)  # no cwd=Path(prism.__file__).parent
findings_verified.suggestion: Pin cwd to the package repo root, record `git status --porcelain` dirtiness, and fall back to the installed package version + file hash rather than bare 'unknown'.
findings_verified.verify_note: The code behaves exactly as claimed. In /home/user/financial-abm-lab/imported/PRISM/src/prism/provenance/tracker.py lines 46-58, get_git_commit() runs `git rev-parse HEAD` with no cwd= argument, so it resolves HEAD of whatever directory the calling process runs in — not the PRISM package. If PRISM were installed as a wheel and run from an unrelated git repo, code_version would silently record that repo's commit; outside any repo, a nonzero returncode falls through to "unknown" without any warning (line 54-58). There is no dirty-tree check. This value is sealed into ProvenanceRecord.code_version (line 97) and into run_id (lines 61-64), and the tracker is wired into PRISM's own pipeline.py, so the path is reachable. Nothing mitigates it: the only test (imported/PRISM/tests/unit/test_provenance.py) doesn't pin cwd semantics. Severity calibration: the flaw is real but confined to imported/PRISM, a retired research lineage; the canonical packages/provenance implementation contains no git-based version capture at all (grep for "git" in packages/provenance returns nothing), so no active/canonical research code inherits this defect. In practice a solo researcher running PRISM from its own repo root gets the correct commit. Downgrading from minor to info: genuine reproducibility weakness, but only in retired code with negligible practical impact.

findings_verified.title: State-machine docstring contradicts _bucket for p50<=F<p90 with backwardation
findings_verified.detail: The module docstring in imported/market-dynamics/state_atlas/online/state_machine.py is internally ambiguous: the ELEVATED rule says "p50 ≤ F < p90 AND (any slope)" while the STRESS rule says "F ≥ p90 OR term_slope < 0 (and not already CALM)" — both claim the mid-band-with-backwardation cell. _bucket resolves it as STRESS (backwardation escalates unless F < p50). Fix is a one-word docstring edit: change "(any slope)" to "AND term_slope ≥ 0". Code behavior appears intended; no test pins the mid-band backwardation case.
findings_verified.evidence:
docstring: 'ELEVATED   p50 ≤ F < p90 AND (any slope)'
code: if F >= atlas.F_p90 or slope < 0: ... return "STRESS"
findings_verified.suggestion: Fix the docstring to match _bucket (or vice versa) and add a test for F in [p50,p90) with slope<0.
findings_verified.verify_note: The code (state_machine.py lines 47-55) returns STRESS for any F >= p50 when slope < 0; only F < p50 with slope < 0 is downgraded to ELEVATED. The docstring's ELEVATED rule (line 5: "p50 ≤ F < p90 AND (any slope)") literally assigns ELEVATED to the mid-band backwardation case, contradicting the code — so the drift the auditor describes is real. However, the auditor's framing overstates it: the docstring's STRESS rule on line 6 ("F ≥ p90 OR term_slope < 0 (and not already CALM)") DOES match the code for that same case. So the docstring is internally ambiguous/overlapping (two rules claim the p50<=F<p90, slope<0 cell) rather than cleanly specifying a different partition. The code resolves the overlap in favor of STRESS. Tests (test_backwardation_triggers_stress_or_elevated per the finding) do not pin the mid-band case, so nothing else disambiguates. Given it is a single ambiguous docstring phrase ("any slope") in an imported non-canonical project (market-dynamics, not the YH speculation-game line), with the correct behavior also documented one line below, this is documentation-only and lower impact than 'likely to bite': info rather than minor.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Loss-conditional ATH hypothesis was formed and 'CONFIRMED' on the same 113 exploration pairs; gate rule revised after seeing v1 data
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The v1 pre-committed gate rule (PROCEED iff disposition CI>0 AND gain-context ATH CI>0) returned YEE_SHARMA_FALLBACK. After the ATH construction fix, run_p0.py's docstring records a new judgement rule ('Judgement updated per Yuito: loss-context ATH>0 -> Stage 2 loss-conditional') adopted after the v1 numbers were known, and P0 PASSes on the loss context of the same exploration ensemble (seeds unchanged, states from S.build_all(cfg)). P0.5 then declares PASS_STAGE2_LOSS_CONDITIONAL_CONFIRMED by re-probing the identical 113 loss pairs (ap_loss filtered from the same exploration split) under 3 clean-probe wordings whose responses are strongly correlated (same states, same model, same prompt family; sec1_faithful reproduces P0's +0.0107 exactly). This is not an independent confirmation — it is a wording-sensitivity check relabeled as confirmation, while the effect magnitude varies 2.7x across wordings (+0.0107 vs +0.0288/+0.0277) and the one out-of-family measurement (behavioral framing) flips sign (-0.0704). The addendum's own discipline ('gate rules frozen before held-out; rules must not be changed after data') is satisfied only in letter because held-out was never touched — which also means the honest confirmation resource (the 40% holdout, or freshly sampled loss pairs) exists and was not used.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: run_p0.py:7-10: 'Judgement updated per Yuito: loss-context ATH>0 (CI lo>0) -> ... PASS'; run_p0_5.py:69: ap_loss = [r for r in coll["ath_pairs"]["exploration"] if r["meta"]["gain_sign"] == "loss"]; gate_v3.json: sec1_faithful +0.0107 == P0's loss value on the same pairs
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Before Stage 2, replicate the loss-conditional ATH sign on freshly sampled loss pairs (new ensemble seed) or a small pre-registered slice, and label P0.5 as a wording-robustness check rather than a confirmation; reserve 'CONFIRMED' for out-of-sample evidence.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Active fingerprint atlas treats the PRISM toy adapters as three distinct literature models, contradicting PRISM's own FATAL-3 finding that they share one skeleton
ideas_research_gaps.detail: notebooks/atlas_v1..v4 gate.json run 12 LHS-sampled simulations each for chiarella_iori, zero_intelligence, and franke_westerhoff alongside genuinely faithful models (SG/CB/LM/MG/GCMG), and MODEL_BOUNDS' comment claims bounds 'bracket the regime where each model has been documented to produce its hallmark stylized facts (see each model's reference)'. But imported/PRISM/docs/AUDIT_REPORT.md FATAL-3 establishes these adapters are near-identical variants of one linear-impact skeleton (d_fund + d_chart + d_noise → price_impact*excess_demand): the CI 'order book' is two scalars with no queue/matching ('docstring…虚偽'), and FW lacks the defining Franke-Westerhoff switching index (plus an undocumented `abs(returns[t])*100` magic scaling at fw.py:196). Their fingerprints in the atlas are therefore fingerprints of the same toy mechanism under three literature names, and cluster-distance conclusions between e.g. 'chiarella_iori' and 'franke_westerhoff' vs real data risk being read as statements about the 2009/2012 papers. backbone_parity.md acknowledges CI/ZI/FW are 'PRISM adapter名…FW系' but the atlas artifacts themselves carry no such caveat.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: adapters.py: 'Bounds bracket the regime where each model has been documented to produce its hallmark stylized facts (see each model's reference)' vs AUDIT_REPORT FATAL-3: '4つのABMが構造的に同一モデル…オーダーブック全体が欠落'
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Rename or flag these entries (e.g. ci_toy/fw_toy/zi_toy or a 'fidelity: reduced-form' field propagated into gate.json and atlas figures), or replace them with faithful implementations before drawing cross-model fingerprint conclusions.

ideas_research_gaps.title: All parity evidence is self-parity: recorded targets and 'original' comparators come from the same Python lineage; no test pins behavior to independent ground truth
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The RECORDED dict was generated by YH005's own Python implementation (outputs/baseline_metrics.json), and the packaged SG is a byte-identical port of that code — docs/backbone_parity.md line 25 itself notes the 0.00% error comes from 'byte-identical 移植'. Likewise test_classical_parity.py and test_prism_family_parity.py compare packages/ copies against the imported/ files they were copied from. So the entire parity suite verifies 'the copy equals the original copy', never 'the implementation equals the published model'. Concretely: the known strategy-review tie-break divergence from the dissertation Java reference (fresh 50% coin vs incumbent-keeps-seat, ~3.5% of closes) is invisible to every test — no test encodes the intended tie-break semantics at all, in packages/abm_models/sg, YH005, or YH006 (speculation_agent.py:364 `if self.active_idx not in best:` re-implements the incumbent-keeps-seat rule, untested). A future 'fix' toward the Java semantics in one implementation would break bit-parity with no test explaining which behavior is correct.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add at least one ground-truth anchor: (a) a unit test on the strategy-review step that constructs a tied-G state and asserts the chosen tie-break rule, with a comment citing the dissertation divergence, so the semantics are pinned and documented in code; (b) if Java reference outputs (or published dissertation figures/metrics) can be obtained, commit them as a fixture and add a statistical-equivalence test against them, clearly separated from the self-parity tests.

ideas_research_gaps.title: No null model for the 'funnel' itself: positive corr(|DG|, h) and a widening quantile fan are generic under diffusive cognitive prices, and the existing Null B (random-decision) machinery was never run through the funnel/bin_var pipeline
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The entire YH006/YH006_1 program treats the funnel (bin_var_slope, rho(|DG|, h), q90-q10 slope diff) as the signature of SG's dynamic-wealth mechanism, and interprets its LOB attenuation as mechanism destruction. But |DG| = |a*(P(t)-P(t0))| where P is a near-random-walk cognitive price, so E|DG| grows like sqrt(h) for ANY agent behavior: a positive rho(|DG|, h) (YH005_1: +0.416, findings.md:108) and an opening q90-q10 fan are what pure diffusion produces with zero wealth mechanism. Conversely, Var(log|DG| | h) under a diffusive null with q independent of h is CONSTANT in h, so the observed negative bin_var_slope is the part that needs a benchmark. No diffusive/analytic benchmark, no shuffled-P control, and no random-decision (decision_mode='random', already implemented in YH005 simulate) run of the 2x2 funnel metrics exists anywhere in the plans. Without it, 'LOB makes the funnel 5x shallower' cannot be decomposed into 'mechanism destroyed' vs 'horizon distribution and diffusion statistics changed'.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: SPECv2.0.md:98 'Bin variance: h を log-equal な K=15 ビンに切り、各ビン内で Var(log|ΔG|) を計算 ... これが funnel 本体'; findings.md:108 'ΔG vs horizon: corr(|ΔG|, τ) = +0.416。明確な漏斗形'; no stage in plans/stage_S1..S6 computes a null-model funnel.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Cheap, high-value experiment: (1) run C0u/C2/C3 with the existing decision_mode='random' (Null B) and compute the identical 5 funnel metrics; (2) add a shuffled/parametric diffusion benchmark for bin_var_slope and q90_q10_slope_diff (simulate P as a random walk with the empirically matched h-distribution and the empirical horizon distribution of each condition). Report all condition values as differences from their null. This directly addresses the most likely examiner attack on the headline.

ideas_research_gaps.title: 'LOB freezes agent turnover' headline compares hazard per calendar step across worlds whose trade rates differ by ~1000x; no event-time (per-round-trip) hazard or activity-matched control
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Bankruptcy (w < B) can only trigger at round-trip close events, and the dossier itself states the mechanical path: '約定が rare → sg_wealth が動かない → bankruptcy 判定に到達しない' (line 179-181). LOB round-trip rate is ~1000x lower than aggregate (findings.md:147 '÷10^3'; S5.5 plan line 29 '~1,000x'). So a per-step hazard of ~3e-3 (agg) vs <=6e-7 (LOB) is largely a predictable arithmetic rescaling of hazard-per-close by trade rate, yet it is sold as the headline discovery ('hazard 構造、τ 不変', §6.2). S5.7 matched the time WINDOW (tau=1499) and S5.8 extended T, but neither matches ACTIVITY. The scientifically informative estimand — hazard per round-trip (event-time KM with clock = cumulative closes per agent), or equivalently whether wealth-per-trade dynamics differ — is never computed. If per-close hazard is equal across worlds, the finding reduces to 'LOB reduces trading', which is true but far weaker than 'LOB freezes the exit mechanism'.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: Dossier §6.2 headline: 'LOB は τ≲250 の初期 shake-out 後に hazard が検出限界以下 (≤6×10⁻⁷) へ凍結し、population の 72–91% が残存し続ける' vs §5.3: '凍結の機械的経路: 約定が rare → sg_wealth が動かない → bankruptcy 判定 (w < B) に到達しない'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Post-processing only (no new sims): recompute KM survival in event time (x-axis = number of closed round-trips per agent) for C0u/C0p/C2/C3 from existing round_trips parquet. If curves collapse, reframe the claim as 'LOB suppresses trade rate; per-trade exit dynamics are conserved'; if they do not collapse, the freeze claim becomes much stronger. Also consider an aggregate arm with a Bernoulli fill-probability throttle matched to the LOB fill rate as a friction-without-book null.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Cognitive threshold anchoring differs qualitatively between worlds: aggregate uses absolute C=3.0 (~10^3 x sd of Δp, so h=±2 is unreachable and h ≈ sign(Δp)); LOB uses c_ticks = 3x median|Δmid| (±2 states live) — the world comparison confounds market mechanism with information structure
ideas_research_gaps.detail: In the aggregate arm, std(r)=3.26e-3 (findings.md:85, N=1000; N=100 is at most ~10x larger) while C=3.0, so |Δp|>C essentially never occurs: the quinary alphabet degenerates to {-1,0,+1} and agents effectively see only the sign of the move. In the LOB arm, c_ticks is calibrated to 3x the MEDIAN |Δmid| (SPEC.md:73), so |Δmid|>c_ticks happens routinely and h=±2 states carry information. The two worlds therefore run on different effective cognitive alphabets and different strategy-table utilization — a confound orthogonal to matching friction, on top of the acknowledged P2 circularity (c_ticks calibrated on SG-free C1 prices, dossier §9.1). Neither the empirical h-distribution per condition nor an alphabet-matched calibration is reported anywhere.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: SPEC.md:72-74: 'h(t) = quantize(Δmid_t, C_ticks) ... C_ticks = 3 × median(|Δmid|) を C1 warm-up run で較正'; findings.md:85: 'std(r)=3.26e-3' with C=3.0; dossier §9.1 admits only the SG-free-calibration half of the problem.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Post-processing: tabulate the empirical distribution of h ∈ {-2..+2} for every condition (aggregate and LOB) from existing h_series. If they differ materially, add a quantile-matched calibration arm: choose c_ticks so that P(h=±2) and P(h=0) match the aggregate baseline (likely c_ticks at a high quantile of |Δmid|), and rerun a few LOB seeds. This also subsumes the planned S5.9 one-pass recalibration.

ideas_research_gaps.title: LOB-migration attribution gap: the 'world effect' bundles matching friction with (i) a 30-agent MMFCN ecology present only in the LOB arm and (ii) fill-selection of which round-trips complete; S5.5/S5.6 control sample size and MMFCN volume, not composition or selection
ideas_research_gaps.detail: YH005→YH006 changes several things at once. N was controlled (the -0.56→-1.93 alpha correction), and S5.5 matched RT counts, S5.6 scanned MMFCN orderVolume. But two confounds remain uncontrolled: (1) the aggregate world contains 100 SG agents only, while the LOB world's price is co-produced by 30 MMFCN agents — 'world effect' could partly be 'presence of exogenous liquidity traders in the price-formation loop', and no aggregate+noise-demand control (or LOB-without-MMFCN, impossible since sg_only gives 0% fill per findings.md:158) exists to separate them; (2) ~30% of SG opens are zero-fill (findings.md:165: 'SG 信号の 3 割が LOB で消失'), so LOB round-trips are a SELECTED subsample conditioned on liquidity state and order size — S5.5's RT10k control subsamples aggregate RTs by open_t order (stage_S5.5_plan.md:52), which matches count but not the selection mechanism. Fill-selection alone could flatten bin_var_slope if large-q or long-horizon trades fail disproportionately. Additionally the funnel's own T-dependence in the LOB is unmeasured (dossier §9.3 admits S5.8's 12 trials are insufficient).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: Dossier §9.4: '単一市場・単一資産、MMFCN 30 体の specific な流動性生態系。一般化可能性は…課題'; findings.md:165: 'zero-fill open 率 ≈ 30% (流動性不足) → SG 信号の 3 割が LOB で消失'; stage_S5.5_plan.md:52: 'aggregate_RT10k: 各 trial の open_t 昇順で最初 10,000 RT を抽出'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Two targeted controls: (a) in the aggregate model, add an exogenous demand term statistically matched to MMFCN net flow (isolates 'other traders' from 'matching engine'); (b) from LOB logs, model the fill/selection propensity (by q, wealth, book state at open) and reweight or subsample AGGREGATE round-trips by that propensity before computing funnel metrics — if reweighted aggregate reproduces the LOB funnel, the effect is selection, not mechanism change.

ideas_research_gaps.title: 'F1 interaction is truly null, not underpowered' overstates the evidence: the ensemble interaction CI is [-0.042, +0.002], nearly excluding 0 on the negative side, and the quoted precision (agg CI width 0.003) is the wrong arm
ideas_research_gaps.detail: findings.md:199 reports the ensemble interaction rho_pearson = -0.020 [-0.042, +0.002]. This decisively refutes the Phase-1 magnitude (-0.27) but is fully consistent with a small negative interaction of ~-0.02 to -0.04; by the project's own P5 note (LOB SD ≈ 0.087, CI halves at 400 trials), 400 LOB trials would likely make it significant. The dossier's justification — '真に null であって underpowered ではない: agg 側 CI 幅 0.003 の精度で 0 を跨ぐ' — cites the precision of the aggregate arm, but the interaction estimator's variance is dominated by the LOB arms, so the aggregate arm's tightness is irrelevant to whether the interaction test is powered. A referee will read the CI as 'marginally non-significant small negative effect', not 'confirmed null'.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: Dossier lines 152-154: 'F1 (world × wealth interaction) は ensemble で null … (真に null であって underpowered ではない: agg 側 CI 幅 0.003 の精度で 0 を跨ぐ)'; findings.md:199: 'Interaction (C3−C2)−(C0p−C0u): rho_pearson = −0.020 [−0.042, +0.002]、CI が 0 を跨ぐ'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Rephrase the claim as an equivalence/magnitude statement: 'the Phase-1 effect size of -0.27 is excluded; any residual interaction is bounded by |rho| < 0.042 (95% CI)'. If 'truly null' is to be kept, run a formal equivalence test (TOST) with a pre-declared smallest-effect-of-interest, or allocate the extra trials to the LOB arms per P5 before the proposal is written.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Integer strategy scores make any r_min in [0, 1) behaviorally identical — the atlas LHS sweep of r_min_static (0.0, 0.05) is a degenerate no-op dimension
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Rolling signed scores are integer-valued (sum of T_win ±1 increments, with the parity of T_win), and the activity rule is best_scores > r_min_vec. Therefore every r_min in [0, 1) selects exactly the same active set (score >= 1, or >= 2 for even parity), and packages/fingerprint_atlas/fingerprint_atlas/adapters.py:86 sampling 'r_min_static': (0.0, 0.05) via LHS explores literally one behavior — the apparent parameter diversity in atlas fingerprints along this axis is illusory. The Jefferies et al. Figure-1 x-axis spans r_min in [-T, T]; a meaningful sweep needs integer-spanning ranges (e.g., -4..+8).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: gcmg/model.py:224: active_mask = best_scores > r_min_vec  (best_scores are integer-valued int32 cast to float) combined with fingerprint_atlas/adapters.py:86: "r_min_static": (0.0, 0.05)
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Sweep r_min_static over an integer-resolving range (and document the parity/integrality of rS in the model docstring so future calibration doesn't repeat this).

ideas_research_gaps.title: Fig. 11 acceptance test and 3-model comparison rest on a single seed (n=1) with ad-hoc thresholds and no sampling-error assessment
ideas_research_gaps.detail: run_null_tests evaluates the acceptance criteria (baseline vol-ACF(50) > 0.10, nulls < 0.05) on exactly one seed (default 777), and compare_three_models.py's figure caption asserts 'Only the Speculation Game (right column) reproduces slow decay of |r| ACF and heavy-tailed returns' from one seed per model (seed=123). No seed ensemble, no variability estimate of ACF(50) across runs, and the 0.10/0.05 thresholds are arbitrary (the white-noise 95% band at T=50000 is ±1.96/√T ≈ 0.009, so the null threshold 0.05 is 5x looser than the noise floor). A single-seed pass/fail gate on a stochastic ABM is exactly the kind of evidence a referee asks to see replicated; the YH006_1 pipeline shows the author knows how to do 100-seed ensembles, but the YH005 mechanism-evidence scripts were never upgraded.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
ok_baseline = vol_acf_at_50["baseline"] > 0.10
ok_null_a = abs(vol_acf_at_50["null_a"]) < 0.05
# run once with seed=777; compare_three_models.py:163: "Only the Speculation Game ... reproduces ..." from seed=123
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Loop the three conditions over ~20 seeds, report mean ± SD of vol-ACF(50) per condition, and set the null threshold relative to the white-noise band (e.g. 3×1.96/√T).

ideas_research_gaps.title: No ACF significance bands anywhere in the canonical stylized-facts package; log-log ACF plots extend into pure noise territory
ideas_research_gaps.detail: return_acf/volatility_acf return point estimates only; no function computes the Bartlett/white-noise band ±1.96/√n, and no caller draws one. compare_three_models.py:141 draws an arbitrary 0.05 dashed reference line and plots |ACF| values down to 1e-4 on log-log axes — at T=50000 everything below ~0.009 is statistically indistinguishable from zero, so the visual 'slow decay' tail of the log-log plot partially plots noise as if it were signal (negative-ACF points are even re-plotted as |ρ| in gray, which on a log axis manufactures a spurious power-law-looking noise floor). Claims about slow (power-law) decay of |r| ACF are central to the SG research line, so band-free ACF figures are a standard referee objection.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
for lag in range(1, max_lag + 1):
    prod = x_demeaned[:-lag] * x_demeaned[lag:]
    m = np.nanmean(prod)
    out[lag - 1] = m / var   # no SE / band anywhere
# compare_three_models.py:141: ax.axhline(0.05, color="gray", linestyle=":", ...)
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add a bartlett_band(n, z=1.96) helper to the package, draw it on every ACF figure, and truncate or de-emphasize log-log ACF points below the band.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Legacy 'confirmed' entries (YH001-YH005_1) rest on single seeds with no uncertainty quantification, and several paper-consistency claims are quantitatively unsupported (e.g., Hill 2.54 vs 1.94 called 'within single-trial fluctuation' when it is ~2.4 SE off)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: findings.md is the project's grep-first ground truth, but its pre-YH006_1 sections state paper-consistency as fact from one seed: YH002 (seed=42) Hill 1.92 vs paper 1.93 and kurtosis 16.66 vs paper 135.73 (an 8x gap waved off); YH005 (seed=777) Hill α=4.53 vs paper 3.8 declared '整合' (with T=20000 and a 10% tail, k≈2000 gives SE≈0.10, so 4.53 vs 3.8 is far outside sampling error — the agreement claim silently depends on tail-fraction choice); YH005_1 wealth Hill α=2.54 vs paper 1.94 dismissed as '単一 trial 揺らぎの範囲' although k≈100 (xmin=p90, N=1000) gives SE≈0.25, i.e., a ~2.4σ discrepancy. The Phase-2 ensemble discipline (100 trials, bootstrap CIs) was applied only to YH006-line metrics; the YH005_1 hypothesis 'does Hill α converge to 1.94 over 100 trials?' (line 125) was left unresolved when the program pivoted to N=100/LOB. These point estimates are what future YHs will grep and trust.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: findings.md:106: 'Wealth Pareto: Hill α (xmin=p90) = 2.54 (論文2 Fig. 4: 1.94)。単一 trial 揺らぎの範囲、[1.5, 3] 内'; findings.md:85: 'Hill α=4.53。論文1 Fig. 4 の α≈3.8 と整合'; findings.md:28: kurtosis '16.66 (論文 Set I の Table 2 値 135.73 には届かないが…'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: A one-day retrofit: rerun YH005 baseline and YH005_1 wealth-Pareto at 10-20 seeds (cheap at aggregate cost, ~100s/run), attach mean ± CI to every number in findings.md, and annotate each legacy single-seed claim with 'point estimate, seed=X, unreplicated'. Resolve or explicitly retire the open 'α→1.94?' hypothesis at line 125.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Quantization boundary dp == ±C is exactly reachable but the boundary convention is untested
ideas_research_gaps.detail: quantize_price_change assigns h=+1 for 0 < dp <= C and h=-1 for -C <= dp < 0 (docstring claims 論文 Eq.6 の不等号境界通り). Since dp = D/N with integer D, dp == C is exactly representable whenever D == C*N (e.g. D=3000, N=1000, C=3.0), so the <= vs < convention is a live code path that changes the history index mu and hence every strategy lookup afterwards. No test pins these boundaries, and a well-meaning refactor to e.g. np.sign(dp)*np.minimum(2, ceil(|dp|/C)) could silently flip them. The dissertation Java's boundary convention would also be worth checking explicitly given the already-known tie-break divergence shows the Java reference differs in small details.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add unit tests for quantize_price_change at dp in {C, -C, 0, C+eps, -C-eps, tiny±} and cross-check the boundary convention against the Java Sample.java.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Virtual-position handling at strategy switch (discard without booking dG) is another Java-fidelity assumption that is documented but never verified
ideas_research_gaps.detail: When the review switches to a new best strategy j** that currently holds a virtual position, both implementations clear v_pos/v_ep/v_ea without booking the virtual round-trip gain ('G は更新しない'), and the outgoing active strategy re-enters the virtual pool flat. This is exactly the kind of underdetermined micro-rule where the already-confirmed tie-break divergence arose (dissertation Java vs all Python SGs). If the Java reference instead settles the virtual position's dG at switch time (or lets it ride), G trajectories — and therefore switch frequencies and stylized facts — would differ systematically. Given the repo keeps papers/katahira_dissertation_code/Sample.java next to YH005, this rule can be checked directly; currently only the docstring asserts it.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
packages/abm_models/abm_models/sg/reference.py:250-255:
                new_idx = int(rng.choice(best))
                # 新 j** に virtual がある → クリア (G は更新しない)
                a.virtual_positions[new_idx] = 0
                a.virtual_entry_prices[new_idx] = 0
                a.virtual_entry_actions[new_idx] = 0
                a.active_idx = new_idx
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Audit Sample.java for the switch-time virtual-position rule (settle vs discard vs carry) and record the verdict next to the tie-break note; if it differs, quantify the impact like the tie-break analysis.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Untrusted arXiv abstracts flow unfenced into the extraction prompt, and their self-scored relevance propagates into downstream LLM prompts
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The user message is raw f"Title: {title}\n\nAbstract: {abstract}" with no delimiting/hardening, and the extraction model itself assigns relevance_score. A prompt-injecting abstract (an increasingly real phenomenon on arXiv) can (a) set relevance_score=1.0 to guarantee selection into the top-7 proposer context and the methods-draft context, and (b) plant instructions inside mechanism_summary/novelty_signal, which propose.py and methods_draft.py then embed verbatim in their prompts — a second-order injection into the proposal generator whose outputs are auto-executed by `propose_cli auto`. Blast radius is bounded (params are validated against MODEL_BOUNDS keys before execution), but rationale text, references, and the methodology notes DB are all influenceable.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: user_msg = f"Title: {paper['title']}\n\nAbstract: {paper['abstract']}"  # plus relevance_score self-reported by the same call
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Fence the abstract in explicit delimiters with an instruction that the content is data, cap/require corroboration for relevance (e.g. category + keyword prior blended with the LLM score), and treat mechanism_summary as quoted data (not instructions) when composing the proposer context.

ideas_research_gaps.title: LLM provenance gap: proposals and extractions record model id but not prompt version, temperature, or context hash
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The proposals table stores llm_model only; SYSTEM_PROMPT has evolved (v1→current, per _feature_typical_ranges docstring) and temperature is user-settable and mutated by the json_validate_failed retry loop (+0.1 per retry), yet none of this is persisted. analytics.py explicitly frames prediction_error-over-proposal-id as a learning curve ('is the LLM getting better as prompts improve?'), but without prompt/temperature/context provenance, prompt changes are unidentifiable confounders in that curve. Same for literature_methods: extracted_by_model is stored but not EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT version, so re-extraction after a prompt change is indistinguishable from the original. The monorepo already has a provenance package that could supply commit/prompt-hash capture.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: insert_proposal(..., llm_model=groq_model)  # no temperature, no prompt hash, no context snapshot
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add prompt_version/prompt_sha256, effective temperature (post-retry), and a context-summary hash to proposals and literature_methods; stamp with the provenance package like runs.provenance_json already does.

ideas_research_gaps.title: 'Inductive learning disabled' S=1 comparison leaves SG's bankruptcy-substitution and wealth-sizing channels active — the structural attribution is confounded
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The 3-model comparison's headline claim (README §8.3 / figure suptitle: 'with inductive learning disabled … only the Speculation Game reproduces slow decay + heavy tails') sets S=1 in all three models. S=1 does remove strategy switching, but the SG run retains two adaptive mechanisms that MG/GCMG structurally lack: (i) bankruptcy substitution — poorly performing agents are continually resampled with fresh strategies (num_substitutions ≈ 10^5 at these parameters, an evolutionary selection channel that reshapes the strategy population over time), and (ii) wealth-proportional order sizing q=⌊w/B⌋, a multiplicative feedback loop. A referee will argue the comparison shows 'SG's full structure minus strategy switching' vs 'bare MG/GCMG', not a clean isolation of the cognitive/round-trip structure. The suite already has the perfect knob: an ablation arm with substitution disabled (treat w<B agents as frozen, or clamp w) and/or constant q would separate the three candidate mechanisms at negligible implementation cost, and would also strengthen the YH005_1 '3-layer mechanism' narrative with a causal ablation rather than five descriptive figures. Also, the entire comparison is a single seed (123); the README's own note that a seed loop 'suffices' should be exercised for at least ~10 seeds before the claim hardens.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: sg_res = _run_model("SG", sg_simulate, N=N, M=M, S=S, T=T, seed=seed, p0=p0)  # S=1, but bankruptcy substitution + q=w//B remain active; MG/GCMG have neither
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add SG ablation arms: (a) S=1 + substitution off, (b) S=1 + fixed q, (c) both; run ≥10 seeds and report vol-ACF/Hill α distributions.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Acceptance criteria never test lag-1 return autocorrelation, which is significantly positive (+0.09) and violates the stylized fact being claimed
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Cont's first stylized fact is absence of linear return autocorrelation, and the README's baseline table reports only 'ret_acf τ=14 = +0.003, noise zone ✓'. But the committed metrics show ret_acf(1) = +0.0917 at T=20000 (baseline_metrics.json) and +0.0648 at T=50000 (null_tests_metrics.json) against a 1.96/√T noise band of ±0.014/±0.009 — a ~7σ positive lag-1 autocorrelation, i.e. the SG price is short-horizon momentum-predictable in a way real returns are not (and the nulls swing to −0.17/−0.12, also unremarked). Checking only τ=14 is selective: the acceptance framework (PLAN §8.4, README table) certifies 'noise zone' at the one lag where it holds. Whether the original Katahira model shows the same lag-1 structure is exactly the kind of quantitative discrepancy the YH-series exists to document — worth a row in the table and a sentence, and worth checking against paper 1's Fig. 5 before YH007 builds on 'stylized facts reproduced'.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: baseline_metrics.json: "ret_acf": {"1": 0.0917, "14": 0.0029, ...}; README table reports only 「ret_acf τ=14 | +0.003 | noise zone ✓」
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Report ret_acf(1) in the README tables, add it to the acceptance discussion (e.g. |acf(1)| threshold or an explicit documented deviation), and compare with 論文1's return-ACF figure.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Headline 2x2 interaction claim rests on one seed and n_tail=10 Hill fits — and must be re-established after the execution-layer fix
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The central claim ('funnel structure robust to Pareto init in aggregate world, destroyed in LOB': corr interaction -0.27; alpha_hill -1.93 world effect) is derived from a single seed=777 run per condition with Hill alpha estimated on 10 tail points; the README itself flags this and defers bootstrap CIs to Phase 2. Two additions a reviewer would demand: (1) since finding #1 shows the LOB-side dynamics are contaminated by runaway phantom positions, the world-axis and interaction effects must be re-measured after the reconcile fix before any of the 2x2 numbers are quotable — the direction of the LOB effects may survive, but the magnitudes (and possibly corr(|dG|,h)=0.61 for C2, which exceeds both aggregate values) are suspect; (2) cheap robustness is available now: the LOB runs take ~15s each, so a 20-50 seed ensemble with the paired (same-seed) design would give sign-level confidence on the interaction without waiting for Phase 2 infrastructure. Also consider reporting the desync/partial/zero-fill diagnostics per condition as first-class columns, since they quantify how far each condition is from the 'real round trip' semantics the comparison assumes.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: README.md:93-94 'α_hill の tail サンプル数 n_tail=10 ... 有意性は Phase 2 で 100 trial bootstrap CI を取るまで保留' and :134-139 (interaction claim); run times README.md:193-195 ('C2 13 s + C3 15 s') make an ensemble immediately feasible.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: After fixing finding #1: rerun the 2x2 over >=20 seeds, report paired-seed interaction distributions, and add per-condition execution-fidelity diagnostics (desync count, partial/zero-fill rates, final |inventory|) to the metrics JSON.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Prompt-sensitivity handling only sweeps the closing instruction, though the run itself demonstrated the instruction body also moves the decision
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The wording-sensitivity apparatus (CLEAN_ANSWER_VARIANTS, Stage 0.2 sweep, P0.5 robustness) varies only the closing answer instruction while PREMISE + INSTRUCTION + information block are held fixed. But REPORT.md's own footnote (§2.3 補足) records that an early de-risk run got P(sell)=0.466 because it used a shortened instruction body — i.e., 'instruction 本文も decision に効く' — and no body-level sensitivity sweep was ever added. Meanwhile the loss-ATH effect that gates Stage 2 varies 2.7x in magnitude across just the three closing wordings tested (+0.0107 / +0.0288 / +0.0277) and is only ~1-3pp on the P(sell) scale, versus a 26pp framing effect from the answer format alone — the measured phenomenon is an order of magnitude smaller than known instrument sensitivities. A peer reviewer would ask for (a) at least one paraphrase of the INSTRUCTION/information body in the robustness family, and (b) state-level covariate analysis (does the per-pair ATH diff correlate with nearness, |unrealized_gain|, remaining_time?) before steering v_ATH on a ~1pp effect.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: REPORT.md §2.3: '補足: … instruction 本文も decision に効くことが判明 (→ wording 感度の所在は closing だけでない)' — yet CLEAN_ANSWER_VARIANTS (render.py:171-193) varies only the closing block; REPORT_v3 loss-ATH means 0.0107/0.0288/0.0277
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add 1-2 neutral paraphrases of the instruction body to the sensitivity family before Stage 2, and report per-pair effect-vs-covariate diagnostics so the tiny mean effect is shown to be structured rather than instrument noise.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Research gap: the corr≈0.33 decoupling guard is measured at a single window size; the claim is scale-dependent and needs a corr-vs-aggregation curve
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Finding 0002 rests on corr(Δmid, net-flow) ≈ 0.33 measured at exactly one aggregation scale (events_per_window=25, the hardcoded default in simulate_lob). Price-impact reasoning — and the repo's own channel_band result, where full aggregation gives corr=1.000 — implies this correlation rises toward 1 as windows lengthen (net flow eventually eats through the book) and falls toward 0 at the single-event scale. So '板は脱共役する' is a statement about one unexamined intermediate scale, and a mechanism reading either channel at a horizon of h windows faces a different effective decoupling than the probe measured. A reviewer would ask for corr as a function of window size (and of p_market/offset_mean, which set the depth-vs-flow ratio) before accepting 0.33 as structural rather than a tuning artifact. The planned M1/M2 rank test (doc item 2) will inherit whatever scale is chosen, so pinning the scale-dependence first is cheap insurance.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: | **corr(Δmid, net-flow)**（8 seed） | **0.329 ± 0.025** |  -- measured only at events_per_window=25 (toy/orderbook.py:61)
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add a small sweep: corr(Δmid, net-flow) for events_per_window in {5, 25, 100, 400} × tick × p_market, and state in the finding at which scales decoupling holds — converting the guard from a point estimate into a regime map.

ideas_research_gaps.title: KDE bandwidth and basin statistics assume iid samples; daily latent trajectories are strongly autocorrelated
ideas_research_gaps.detail: gaussian_kde with Scott's rule sets bandwidth ~ n^(-1/6) using the raw sample count, but z(t) is a daily trajectory with multi-week autocorrelation, so the effective sample size is far smaller than n. The density is systematically undersmoothed, seeding extra local minima, and the persistence diagram / basin counts (the headline Phase 4.5 measurement) inherit an optimism bias that the min_persistence=1.0 merge only partially absorbs. Nowhere is uncertainty attached to n_basins or the persistence spectrum. The same stationarity assumption underlies F = -log(rho): a 15-year pooled KDE mixes structurally different epochs (pre/post-2018 vol regime, ZIRP vs hiking), so basins may be historical epochs rather than recurrent regimes.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: kde = gaussian_kde(pts.T, bw_method=bandwidth)  # bandwidth from raw n, samples autocorrelated
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Scale bandwidth by effective sample size (n_eff from the integrated autocorrelation time), and put block-bootstrap (moving-block over time) confidence bands on the persistence diagram and basin counts.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Batch-vs-continuous learning comparison confounds mechanism with reward density per Q-update (N x more events per learning period)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Under D-B3, one learning period = one clearing cycle, so a batch-N market maker receives the aggregate of N market steps (N x expected fills, N x jump variance) in every reward/Q-update, while the continuous MM gets a single sparse step. Finding 0002 establishes that convergence and collusion learnability in this harness are SNR-limited precisely in per-period fill probability (gap/noise ~ sqrt(pn)/sqrt(lr)); batch N=20 at the same nu therefore sits in a mechanically better learning-statistics regime than continuous. Any batch-minus-continuous markup difference - including delta_GP measured in the revisable ablation, which is interpreted as the 'Green-Porter monitoring' channel - mixes auction economics with pure reward-densification. D-B3 explicitly rejected fixed-real-time periods because it would contaminate the comparison with learning frequency, but the chosen convention has the mirror confound and no control cell isolates it (e.g., continuous with nu scaled by N to match per-period event counts, or batch with rewards renormalized per step).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: def step_core(...):\n    n_steps = cfg.period_steps  # continuous=1, batch=N; one reward and one Q-update per period regardless (env.py:99-107, learnconfig.py:111-114)
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add an event-density-matched control to the density spoke (continuous at nu*N vs batch-N at nu), or report per-period reward SNR alongside markup so the attribution delta_GP can be bounded against the learning-statistics channel.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Same seeds 1000-1099 reused across all conditions (common random numbers) but bootstrap_interaction_ci resamples conditions independently — inconsistent with the paired estimator used elsewhere
ideas_research_gaps.detail: config.py sets ENSEMBLE_SEED_BASE=1000/ENSEMBLE_N_TRIALS=100 for every condition, and in the aggregate world C0u and C0p with the same seed share identical strategy tables, initial history and activity draws (only wealth init diverges) — an implicit common-random-numbers pairing. The codebase then treats this pairing inconsistently: stats.bootstrap_interaction_ci resamples the four conditions independently (lines 55-60), discarding the pairing and overstating the variance of the interaction, while aggregate_full_summary.compute_interactions builds per-trial paired interactions and bootstraps those — a paired estimator. Two different estimators for the same estimand, with different power, coexist without a stated rationale. A reviewer would ask: is CRN pairing across conditions intended (then use the paired bootstrap everywhere and document it) or not (then give each condition a disjoint seed range, e.g. via SeedSequence.spawn per condition)?
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
for i in range(n_resample):
        b_C3 = rng.choice(rho_C3, size=rho_C3.size, replace=True).mean()
        b_C2 = rng.choice(rho_C2, ...).mean()  # independent resampling
...
config.py:117-118: ENSEMBLE_SEED_BASE = 1000; ENSEMBLE_N_TRIALS = 100  # same range for every cond
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Decide explicitly: (a) keep shared seeds as a variance-reduction design and use seed-matched paired bootstrap everywhere (resampling seed indices jointly across the 4 conditions), or (b) allocate disjoint per-condition seed ranges via SeedSequence spawning. Document the choice in the experiment brief.

ideas_research_gaps.title: YH006 _reconcile edge cases (zero-fill open, partial open/close, close-cancel, bankruptcy substitution) have no direct tests
ideas_research_gaps.detail: _reconcile is the semantic heart of YH006 — it decides how LOB fills map back onto SG-cognitive state (position, entry_quantity, G update, sg_wealth, strategy review, substitution). Its six branches (open: zero/partial/full; close: full/partial/self-cancelled) are reachable only stochastically through full pams simulations, and the three tests that exist assert either determinism (test_parity), loose aggregates (test_roundtrip_invariants, 50% tolerance) or tautologies (test_wealth_conservation). None of: a partial open reducing entry_quantity, a zero-fill open incrementing num_zero_opens and resetting entry state, a partial close keeping position with shrunken quantity, the close-cancel branch (actual_vol == entry_quantity), or _substitute triggering when sg_wealth < B, is ever asserted directly. This is exactly where YH005-to-YH006 semantic drift would hide, and it interacts with the (unpinned) tie-break rule re-implemented at line 364.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add unit tests that instantiate SpeculationAgent with a stubbed market/prng, set pending_intent/entry state directly, vary asset_volumes, and assert the post-state of each branch: counters, position, entry fields, G/sg_wealth deltas, round_trips entries, strategy-review outcome on tie and non-tie, and _substitute firing at sg_wealth < B. These need no pams runner and would run in milliseconds.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Headline survival gap '52x/58x' is a ratio of two tiny KM tail probabilities reported without a CI on the ratio itself
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The headline S(1499) ratios gap_u = S_C2(1499)/S_C0u(1499) and gap_p are computed from pooled point estimates, with max(., 1e-12) guarding the denominator. Per-condition bootstrap CIs exist (TAU_GRID includes 1499), but no CI is ever computed for the ratio, and a ratio of a large numerator to a tail probability estimated near zero (agg censoring ~25%, S(1499) small) is extremely unstable — the same seed-level multinomial bootstrap already implemented in bootstrap_km_ci could bootstrap the log-ratio directly at negligible cost. Quoting '52x/58x' with curve attachment (per the note field) but no interval is exactly where a referee will push.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
gap_u = headline["C2"] / max(headline["C0u"], 1e-12)
gap_p = headline["C3"] / max(headline["C0p"], 1e-12)
... f" | gap uniform={gap_u:,.0f}x pareto={gap_p:,.0f}x"
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Bootstrap log(S_lob(1499)/S_agg(1499)) with paired seed resampling (both conditions share seeds) inside the existing bootstrap_km_ci machinery and report the interval next to the point ratio.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Rejecting paired seeds / common random numbers for the wealth contrast throws away exactly the variance reduction the project later diagnosed it needs (P5: LOB SD 0.087, 400 trials to halve the CI)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: SPECv2 §5.1 chooses independent seeds per condition for 'implementation simplicity', explicitly reverting the v1.0 paired-seed design (revision history line 397). But the uniform-vs-Pareto contrast within a world differs ONLY in the initial wealth draw; with a shared seed for strategies, history, and MMFCN behavior, the trial-level difference rho(C3_i) - rho(C2_i) would cancel most seed variance, plausibly cutting the interaction CI by 2-4x at zero simulation cost. The project then hit precisely this wall: P5 concludes LOB trial SD ≈ 0.087 requires ~400 trials for a half-width CI, and the interaction verdict (CI [-0.042, +0.002]) is left ambiguous. The RNG architecture (single default_rng stream) makes true CRN nontrivial, but a split-stream design (one stream for wealth init, one for everything else) is a small refactor with a parity test.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: SPECv2.0.md:132: '各条件 100 trial を独立 seed で実行 (paired seed は採用しない)。実装上の単純さと条件比較の頑健性を優先。100 trial 規模では検出力は十分。' — contradicted by dossier P5: 'LOB は SD≈0.087 で CI 半減に 400 必要'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: For any follow-up (S1-secondary, YH006_2): split the RNG into (wealth-init stream, dynamics stream) keyed by (seed, condition-invariant), verify uniform-mode parity, and rerun the wealth contrast as a paired design. Report the paired-difference CI; if it excludes 0 the interaction question reopens cheaply, and if not, the 'truly null' claim gains the power it currently lacks.

ideas_research_gaps.title: A1 ablation's q_const is calibrated from C3 pilots (median of a Pareto-tailed q), so C2_A1/C3_A1 change total order flow relative to their parents by different amounts — ablation deltas confound 'q heterogeneity removed' with 'aggregate volume shifted'
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Appendix A.1 fixes q_const = median over agents and time of q in C3 (LOB+Pareto) pilots. Under Pareto α=1.5 initial wealth the median q is far below the mean, so setting every agent to q_const reduces C3's total submitted volume (tail agents lose their large orders) while for C2 (uniform, q∈{1..12}) the shift is different in sign/magnitude. Since fill rates and price impact in the LOB depend on total volume vs MMFCN supply, the ablation contrast Δ_A1 = rho(C*_A1) − rho(C*) mixes the intended channel (q heterogeneity) with an unintended one (liquidity-consumption level). This matters for the asymmetric key result (C2_A1 restores the funnel to −0.31 while C3_A1 stays at −0.09, dossier §4.2) that motivates 'hypothesis A revised': the asymmetry could partly reflect different volume displacement rather than a distinct Pareto-specific mechanism. Only one q_const value was ever run.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: SPECv2.0.md:353-357: 'C3 … の設定で 5 trial の pilot run … median_{t,i}(q_i(t)) … 5 trial 分の median のさらに median を取る → これを q_const として固定'; dossier §4.2: 'C2_A1 −0.3071 … C3_A1 −0.0901 q 固定でも動かず'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add a small q_const scan (e.g., {median, mean-matched, per-condition-volume-matched}) at ~20 trials per point on C3_A1 only, and record fill rates: if Δ_A1(C3) is invariant to volume-matched q_const, the 'separate pathway' interpretation survives; if not, the S5/S6 causal story needs a volume covariate.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Multiplicity control (Bonferroni α=0.0125 over 4 pre-registered tests) was defined for S1-S6 but the S5.5-S5.8 extensions added at least four new stage-local hypothesis tests (H_micro, H_artifact, H_frozen, survival ratios) with no family-wise accounting for the final claim set
ideas_research_gaps.detail: SPECv2 §5.4 fixes the testing family at 4 (Spearman main + A1 + A3 + bin variance). The subsequent unplanned-but-preregistered stages each introduced their own decision thresholds (S5.5 |slope| gates, S5.6 elasticity ε≤0.3, S5.7 matched-KM comparisons, S5.8 hazard UB 2e-5), and the dossier's proposal-grade claim list (§5.1, four 'confirmed' items) draws on essentially all of them. Each stage's per-test discipline is good (thresholds fixed before looking), but the accumulated family across the stage sequence is ~10 tests, and the write-up nowhere states how many looks were taken or adjusts the confirmatory claims. A statistics-literate referee will ask whether 'H_frozen 確定' style verdicts survive a hierarchical/global error-rate framing, especially since stages were added adaptively after seeing prior-stage data.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: SPECv2.0.md:147: '…で 4 系統。Bonferroni 補正で α = 0.05 / 4 = 0.0125 を主検定の閾値とする' vs dossier §4.1 listing S5.5-S5.8 verdicts (H_micro 強支持 / H_artifact_negated_strong / H_frozen 確定) each with its own stage-local threshold.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: In the proposal's methods section, present the full decision tree (which tests were planned when, which were data-dependent additions) and label S5.5+ results as sequential confirmatory analyses with per-stage α, or apply a simple global correction to the headline p-values/CIs.
