The plan→diff paper trail already exists in plans/ — surfacing it is cheap and preempts the critique.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Acceptance thresholds are adjusted after seeing the data (YH005 baseline ACF gate relaxed 0.15→0.10 post-measurement; JSON still records the failed gate), and ACF 'null destroyed' thresholds are round numbers rather than significance bands
ideas_research_gaps.detail: YH005_PLAN §8.2 pre-set the null-test acceptance 'baseline |r| ACF(50) > 0.15'; the run produced 0.119, outputs/null_tests_metrics.json faithfully records "baseline_gt_0.15": false, and README.md then relabels the criterion '> 0.10' as a revision of a 'rough plan-stage estimate'. The relabeling is transparent (good), but it is the same outcome-dependent-criterion pattern as the YH008 gate redefinition, and findings.md carries the result forward without noting the gate originally failed. Relatedly, the Null A/B destruction criterion |ACF|<0.05 is an arbitrary round number: with T=50000 the Bartlett noise band is ~2/√T ≈ 0.009, so Null A's vol_acf(1)=0.041 'passes' while being ~4.5σ of residual structure — the nulls are weakened, not destroyed, and no significance band is ever drawn on the ACF figures.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: outputs/null_tests_metrics.json: '"acceptance": { "baseline_gt_0.15": false, … }'; README.md:222: '当初の §8.2 baseline > 0.15 は PLAN 段階の粗い見積もり。実測と論文 Fig. 6 の定量値を踏まえて > 0.10 に改訂した。'
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Adopt a house rule: acceptance thresholds may only be revised in a new plan version BEFORE rerunning, and failed gates are reported as failed-then-revised in findings.md. Replace fixed ACF cutoffs with the Bartlett band (±2/√T) or a block-bootstrap CI, and restate the Null A/B result as 'ACF reduced to k× the noise band' rather than pass/fail at 0.05.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Active research line (YH006_1 S6 / survival-gap finding) frozen for ~4 weeks while all effort went to fingerprint_atlas tooling
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The dossier (snapshot 2026-06-07) states the publishable finding is the LOB survival-gap/freeze mechanism and that S6 (A3 lifetime-cap ablation) is implemented and 'Mac 100 trial 待ち', with S1-secondary bootstrap and the plan A/B branch decision explicitly blocking YH006_2 scope. git log shows zero commits to this line since; instead, 19 of the last 20 commits (2026-06-27 onward) build fingerprint_atlas LLM tooling (Groq proposal generator, arXiv ingest, preference learning, CLI polish). The highest-value asset in the repo — a completed 100-trial ensemble with a confirmed novel mechanism one ablation away from a paper — is stalled behind a single overnight compute run, while weeks went into infrastructure for a side project. A strong advisor would say: STOP polishing atlas CLIs, START by running S6 and S1-secondary, make the plan A/B call, and write the YH006 paper.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: dossier line 99: '| S6 | A3 ablation (進行中) | Windows 実装完了 (hook + subclass + τ_max=121 較正 + parity 27 件 PASS)。Mac 100 trial 待ち |'; findings.md line 208: 'S1-secondary を先に完了させる: plan A/B 判定が確定しないと YH006_2 の scope が固まらない'; git log --since=2026-05-25: 19 commits touch packages/fingerprint_atlas, 2 touch imported/speculation-game-info (one is a Java reference add).
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Time-box fingerprint_atlas as 'done for now'. Execute S6 (100 trials) and S1-secondary bootstrap, record the plan A/B decision in the dossier, and draft the YH006 survival-gap paper. Everything atlas-related can wait; the dossier cannot (context decays).

ideas_research_gaps.title: Atlas silhouette collapsed from 0.195 (v1) to 0.0005 (v4) — by the module's own 'sand' rule, the LLM proposal/preference stack was built on an unvalidated foundation
ideas_research_gaps.detail: atlas.py's docstring commands: if the validation gate fails, fix fingerprint.py/MODEL_BOUNDS 'BEFORE building anything on top... if it's sand, every layer above is sand too'. The recorded gates show monotone degradation as synthetic/real refs were added: v1 silhouette_macro=0.1954 (93 runs), v3=0.0166, v3_periods=-0.0625 (ok=false), v4=0.0005 with separation_ratio down to 1.72. A macro-silhouette of 0.0005 means model families barely separate in the 9-D fingerprint space — yet v4 was followed by the preference-learning loop, the Groq proposer (whose atlas_state context is per-family centroids in this near-degenerate space), and novelty scoring, all of which are distance computations in exactly this geometry. The repo already contains the known fix: PROV-ABM-atlas's core idea (SF are degenerate across mechanisms; intervention RESPONSES discriminate) and working apply_intervention()/calibrate_baseline() methods on CI/ZI/FW (packages/abm_models/abm_models/chiarella_iori/model.py:93, _prism_compat.py ModelAdapter protocol). Adding intervention-response axes (e.g. Δvolatility under transaction_tax, Δspread under tick_size change) to the fingerprint is the natural, already-half-implemented way to break the degeneracy — and would finally connect the retired PRISM lineage to the atlas instead of leaving it as dead weight.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: notebooks/atlas_v{1,3,3_periods,4}/gate.json: sil 0.1954 → 0.0166 → -0.0625 (ok=false) → 0.0005; atlas.py:11-13 'If the gate fails ... fix ... BEFORE building anything on top ... if it's sand, every layer above is sand too.'
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Before any further atlas work: either (a) restrict the gate population to the 8 ABM families and report real/synthetic refs as projected landmarks only (silhouette across 23 pseudo-classes with n=1 real windows is statistically meaningless anyway), or (b) extend the fingerprint with intervention-response features using the calibrate/intervene machinery already extracted from PRISM. Option (b) is a genuinely novel paper (SF-fingerprint + intervention-fingerprint fusion) that no one in the Gao et al. line has done.

ideas_research_gaps.title: The advertised INVERSE experiment (which canonical model is nearest to real SPX/BTC?) is never actually computed anywhere
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The package docstring names three goals: ATLAS, NOVELTY, INVERSE ('nearest-neighbour to a target market series'). real_refs.py ingests SPX and BTC with 5 disjoint period windows each, and v3/v4 gate.json confirm those 12 real-market fingerprints sit in the runs DB alongside 96 ABM runs. Yet grep finds no function, CLI, notebook, or doc that answers the inverse question — analytics.py only plots proposal-prediction calibration; atlas.py only computes the intra/inter gate. The single cheapest headline result this infrastructure enables — 'real_spx_p0..p4: nearest family = X, distance = d; does the nearest model change across periods (regime identification)?' — is one distance_matrix lookup away and has never been run or recorded. This is also the only experiment that would justify the atlas to an external reviewer: it converts the geometry from an internal consistency check into a statement about real markets, and it would directly test whether SG (the researcher's own model line) sits closer to real markets than FW/LM.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: __init__.py:10 '* INVERSE = nearest-neighbour to a target market series'; notebooks/atlas_v4/gate.json lists real_spx_full/p0-p4, real_btc_full/p0-p4 with 1 run each; rg 'nearest|inverse' over fingerprint_atlas/*.py matches only docstrings and inverse-variance weighting.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add a ~30-line inverse_report(db) that, for each real_* row, ranks the 8 model-family centroids by distance and writes a table to notebooks/. Report per-period nearest model. If SG wins on any period, that is a marketable sentence for the YH-series papers; if GARCH(1,1) synthetic wins everywhere, that is the honest negative result the v4 axes were designed to probe.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Tie-break sensitivity experiment: the just-committed dissertation Java reference makes the known tie-break divergence testable in one afternoon, but no experiment is planned
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The known finding (not re-reported here) is that all Python SG implementations resolve strategy-review ties by letting the incumbent keep its seat (findings.md design hole 4.1), while the dissertation Java reference flips a fresh 50% coin, and ties occur at ~3.5% of close events. On 2026-07-01 the cleaned Java reference (Sample.java, 350 lines) was committed to YH005/papers/katahira_dissertation_code — i.e., the ground truth is now in-repo — but nothing in findings.md, the dossier, or tests plans the obvious follow-up: a tie_break='coin' backend option in packages/abm_models/sg plus a re-run of the YH005 findings battery (Hill α, kurtosis ladder, vol ACF, null ratios) and the YH005_1 funnel correlation, to measure whether the divergence moves any headline number beyond the 5% parity tolerance. Without this, every YH-series paper carries an unquantified fidelity caveat that a referee who reads the dissertation appendix could raise; with it, the caveat becomes a one-line robustness statement either way. The RNG-consumption-order parity contract makes this the rare case where the sensitivity run is genuinely cheap and decisive.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: findings.md:89 '4.1 argmax tie-break: 現 j* が argmax 集合に含まれれば継続、さもなくば argmax から uniform'; commit 1cec347 (2026-07-01) 'speculation-game: add cleaned Katahira dissertation Java reference ... ground truth for the Python reimplementations'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add an opt-in tie_break parameter to the canonical SG (default preserves bit-parity), run the YH005/YH005_1 metric battery under both rules for ~10 seeds, and record the deltas in findings.md. If deltas are within noise, cite it as robustness; if not, that is itself a publishable sensitivity result about SG's strategy-churn mechanism.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Self-identified 'free' findings never harvested: paper-1 Figs 11-13 are computable from existing YH005_1 logs with zero new simulation
ideas_research_gaps.detail: In 2026-04 the researcher recorded that Katahira-Chen paper-1 Fig. 11 (return asymmetry), Fig. 12 (leverage effect), and Fig. 13 (gain/loss asymmetry) 'post-processing のみで出せる (新規 simulate 不要、round_trips と h_series から計算可能) → Phase 3 で追加予定' — the round_trips dict (10.4M records) and order-size series already exist in the YH006_1 parquet ensembles. Two months later this Phase 3 has not happened, and neither has the other self-flagged cheap check: whether 100-trial averaging moves wealth-Pareto Hill α from the observed 2.54 toward the paper's 1.94 (line 125 lists it, and the 100-trial C0u/C0p ensembles needed for it have since been RUN for YH006_1 — the data exists, only the post-processing is missing). These are the lowest-cost/highest-completeness items in the whole repo: they would upgrade the SG reproduction from 'most stylized facts' to a full 11-fact battery, directly strengthening the YH006 paper's baseline section. Notably, 'leverage' is already computed as a fingerprint feature (fingerprint.py FEATURE_NAMES), so the code for Fig. 12's core statistic exists in-repo too.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: findings.md:125 '100 trial 平均で Hill α が論文値 1.94 に近づくか (Phase 2)'; :127 'Fig. 11 ... Fig. 12 ... Fig. 13 ... は post-processing のみで出せる ... Phase 3 で追加予定'; YH006_1 C0u/C0p = 100-trial aggregate ensembles already on disk (2520 parquet files tracked).
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: One post-processing script over the existing C0u ensemble: Figs 11-13 + 100-trial Hill α. Half a day of work closes two open loops that have been documented as pending since April and pre-empts the 'you only reproduced 10 of 11 facts' referee question.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Cross-model stylized-fact comparison at publication scale is missing: the REGISTRY's obvious deliverable (a canonical 8-model SF table) exists only as reduced-T atlas points in notebook PNGs
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Spec 001 §4 promises 'docs/ (model_catalog, findings統合, ...)' — no model catalog exists. The only cross-model comparison ever run is the fingerprint atlas, which deliberately uses reduced sizes (adapters.py:93 n_steps in (2000,3000); module docstring: 'Sizes are *reduced* ... so the full 8-model × LHS sweep runs in minutes') — adequate for the validation gate, inadequate for citable stylized-fact numbers (Hill α on ~2000 returns with 5% tail = ~100 order statistics; vol-ACF at lag 200-500 is unmeasurable at T=2000 vs the T=20000-50000 used in every YH finding). So the repo can honestly claim '8 parity-verified models behind one protocol' but cannot yet show the one table a reviewer or the researcher's future self needs: model × {Hill α, excess kurtosis ladder, ret-ACF(1), vol-ACF(50/200), leverage} at canonical T with ensemble CIs, computed by the ONE canonical stylized_facts battery. This table is also the honest referee for the atlas: it would show directly which facts separate SG from FW/LM and which are degenerate, without PCA.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: adapters.py:10-13 'Sizes are *reduced* (T≈2000-3000) so the full 8-model × LHS sweep runs in minutes'; spec 001 §4 'docs/ # model_catalog, findings統合' — docs/ contains only adr/0001 and backbone_parity.md; findings.md canonical scales are T=20000-50000.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add experiments/catalog.py: for each REGISTRY model at its canonical params, 20-seed ensemble at canonical T, one packages/stylized_facts summary row each, write docs/model_catalog.md with mean±CI. Roughly one overnight run; becomes the anchor table for every subsequent paper and the ground truth the atlas must reproduce.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Literature DB only feeds the parameter-sweep proposer; it is never pointed at the researcher's own novelty claims (YH006 survival-gap, SG-on-LOB)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: arxiv_ingest builds a structured corpus (mechanism summary, stylized facts targeted, novelty signal per paper) and propose.py consumes it to ground LLM param-sweep proposals for the atlas. But the researcher's actual near-term need is prior-art defense for YH006: the dossier's headline claims ('LOB friction freezes agent turnover', 'wealth-order-size coupling on a LOB') need a demonstration that no existing paper (e.g. in q-fin.TR's MG/SG-on-LOB literature) already reports them — exactly the query this DB was built to answer. Nothing in the codebase or docs runs the corpus against the YH-series: arxiv_cli search exists, but no saved query, no related-work dump, no link from the dossier's §literature to the DB. Two halves of the repo — the LLM literature machine and the dissertation-track research — face away from each other; connecting them costs one CLI invocation per claim and would convert weeks of tooling investment into direct thesis value.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: arxiv_ingest.py docstring: 'builds a queryable literature DB so the proposer (propose.py) can ground its suggestions'; dossier and imported/speculation-game-info/docs/literature.md contain hand-curated citations only, no reference to the fingerprint_atlas literature DB; DEFAULT_QUERIES even includes abs:"speculation game" (financial_abm_strict preset) — the query exists, its results are never used for YH006.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Run the financial_abm_strict ingest, then add a 'related-work audit' section to the YH006 dossier generated from the DB: papers matching {LOB, minority game/speculation game, wealth dynamics, agent survival}. Store the query + date so the novelty claim is reproducible at submission time.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Verified: canonical sg package is byte-identical to YH005 sources except relative-import lines; Null-mode and bankruptcy RNG paths bit-match today
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Positive cross-check result for the parent audit: diff of packages/abm_models/abm_models/sg/{reference,vectorized,history}.py against imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/{model,simulate,history}.py shows only 'from history import ...' -> 'from .history import ...' (history.py identical byte-for-byte). Independent probes confirmed reference/vectorized bit-parity for decision_mode='random' and history_mode='exogenous' (paths not covered by the workspace test), and confirmed the vectorized virtual open-then-close mask ordering cannot self-trigger within a step (a just-opened slot needs rec == -rec). The recorded metrics in tests/test_sg_parity.py match YH005 outputs/baseline_metrics.json digit-for-digit. So the 'YH005 is canonical / byte-identical port' claims in ADR 0001 and docs/backbone_parity.md hold as of today; the risk is purely forward drift (see the two major findings).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: diff output: model.py vs reference.py and simulate.py vs vectorized.py each differ only at the single import line; history.py diff is empty.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: No action beyond adding the drift guards recommended in the two major findings.

ideas_research_gaps.title: excess==0 tie in MG/GCMG is resolved by deterministic history parity but commented as a 'coin flip'
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Market.tick() (and the vectorized loop, line 181-182, and GCMG lines 133-134) resolves a tied attendance with winning_side = 1 if history & 1 else -1 — a deterministic function of the last winner — while the comment calls it a コインフリップ (coin flip). For even N this couples the tie outcome to history parity (the new winner repeats the previous bit, reinforcing history cycles) rather than being unbiased. GCMG's n_active==0 branch, by contrast, uses a real rng.random() draw, so the two tie conventions coexist. Impact is negligible for the recommended odd N (ties impossible in MG; still possible in GCMG where abstention can leave an even active count), but given this fleet's known tie-break finding in the SG lineage, tie conventions deserve accurate comments and a deliberate choice.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Use rng for the excess==0 branch (as the GCMG n_active==0 branch already does) or fix the comment to say 'deterministic parity rule'.

ideas_research_gaps.title: PRISM-family paths hardwire returns[0]=0 and simple (not log) returns, unlike the YH-lineage models
ideas_research_gaps.detail: ZI/CI/FW _simulate_one_path all allocate returns = np.zeros(T) and start the loop at t=1, so element 0 is a structural zero included in every downstream fact computation (a small kurtosis/vol bias at n_steps~1000-3000 atlas scale), and they report simple returns (p_t - p_{t-1})/p_{t-1} while lux_marchesi returns np.diff(np.log(prices)) and base.returns_of() derives log-returns for price-bearing models. Cross-model fingerprint comparisons therefore mix return conventions and a padded zero. Both quirks are inherited verbatim from PRISM and are individually tiny, but they are systematic across all three adapters.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
prices = np.full(T, p.fundamental_value)
returns = np.zeros(T)
for t in range(1, T): ... returns[t] = (prices[t] - prices[t-1]) / prices[t-1]  # returns[0] stays 0.0
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Return returns[1:] (length T-1) and standardize on log-returns across the model zoo (or convert in returns_of).

ideas_research_gaps.title: bootstrap_interaction_ci returns the bootstrap-resample mean as the point estimate instead of the plug-in interaction estimate
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The function returns float(boots.mean()) as the first element, i.e. the mean of the 10,000 resampled interaction statistics, rather than the plug-in estimate (mean(rho_C3)-mean(rho_C2))-(mean(rho_C0p)-mean(rho_C0u)) computed on the original arrays. For the mean-difference statistic these agree in expectation and the discrepancy is O(1/sqrt(n_resample)) Monte-Carlo noise, so no result changes materially, but the reported point estimate carries avoidable bootstrap noise and will not exactly reproduce across n_resample choices even though rng is fixed. bootstrap_ci (line 30) does it correctly (returns arr.mean()). Callers in aggregate_ensemble.py properly dropna before calling, so NaN propagation is handled.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: return float(boots.mean()), float(lo), float(hi)  # stats.py:62 — point estimate from resamples, not from the data
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Return the plug-in interaction estimate as the point value and keep boots only for the percentile CI.

ideas_research_gaps.title: pytest testpaths includes 'packages' but no tests exist under packages/, and CI push triggers omit the active development branch
ideas_research_gaps.detail: pyproject.toml:41 sets testpaths = ["tests", "packages"], but find shows zero test_*.py under any packages/* member — the entry only adds collection time and implies per-package test suites that don't exist. Relatedly, ci.yml:5 triggers push builds only on [main, consolidation]; 'consolidation' does not exist as a branch and the actual work branch (claude/speculation-game-cleanup-4inq82) gets CI only via pull_request, so direct pushes during active research are unchecked. Neither breaks anything today, but both are config statements that no longer describe reality.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: pyproject.toml:41 'testpaths = ["tests", "packages"]'; find packages/*/ -name 'test_*.py' returns nothing; ci.yml:5 'branches: [main, consolidation]' vs git branch -a showing no 'consolidation'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Drop 'packages' from testpaths (or move parity tests next to their packages), and update the push branch list to the branches actually in use.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Early returns (stale_flatten / forced_retire) skip _update_virtual, so non-active strategies miss G updates on those steps
ideas_research_gaps.detail: In YH005/aggregate_sim, virtual open/close bookkeeping runs for every agent every step. In the LOB agent, the stale-flatten path (return at line 234) and the forced-retire path (return at line 247) exit before _update_virtual(mu_t, P_now) at line 311, so on those steps the non-active strategies neither open nor close virtual round trips even though mu advanced. With 200-300 stale_flatten events per run (measured 223-272) the aggregate effect is small, but it is an additional uncontrolled asymmetry between the two worlds being compared, and it grows with exactly the pathologies of finding #1 (more stale fills → more flatten steps → more skipped virtual updates).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: speculation_agent.py:222-234 (stale flatten '...return orders') and :244-247 (forced retire '...return orders') both precede :311 'self._update_virtual(mu_t, P_now)'.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Call _update_virtual before the early returns (mu_t/P_now are already available at that point), keeping virtual bookkeeping step-complete regardless of the real-order path taken.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Verified: S5.7/S5.8 survival stack is correct — KM convention, re-censoring, cluster bootstrap, and zero-event bound all check out
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Because censoring correctness was a review focus: (1) km_from_counts implements the standard discrete KM with censored subjects kept at risk at their censoring time (n_at_risk via reverse cumsum of d+c); I validated it against a hand-computed example ([1,2,2,3c,4] → S = [1,.8,.4,.4,0]) and it matches exactly. (2) The T=1500/T=10000 re-censoring convention (drop t_birth≥T; clip t_end>T with censored=True) is implemented identically in subsample_aggregate.recensor_lifetime_T1500, survival_analysis.load_lifetimes_matched, and survival_extension.load_lifetimes_window, and is additionally guarded by a runtime assertion against the S5.5 censoring rates (0.5%pt stop trigger). (3) bootstrap_km_ci resamples seeds via multinomial weights — a proper cluster bootstrap that respects trial-level dependence, with the pooled-iid Greenwood band explicitly demoted to figure-only. (4) S5.8's rule-of-three 95% hazard upper bound for the zero-event extension window (3/exposure) is the right response to '6 seeds cannot resolve the hazard'. Residual caveats worth one line in the writeup: successive incarnations of an agent slot are treated as independent samples (renewal assumption; affects pooled-KM variance, mitigated by the seed bootstrap), and the S5.5 T1500 RT subsample keeps ~0.2% of round trips whose t_close>1500 which LOB could never observe (I measured 68/31,588 for C0u seed 1000 — negligible).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: n_at_risk = np.flip(np.cumsum(np.flip(total, axis=-1), axis=-1), axis=-1) ... factor = 1 − d/n_at_risk; verified S=[1,.8,.4,.4,0,0] on toy data
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Promote the toy-example check into a unit test (see testing-gap finding) and note the within-slot renewal assumption in the S5.7 methods paragraph.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Playground selftest is sound and passes, but is wired into no test suite and fails under the system python
ideas_research_gaps.detail: I executed _selftest.py: it fails under system python3 (no numpy) but passes fully ('ALL OK', 6/6) with the workspace venv, exercising the real YH001-YH005_1 models plus sf.py. Its claim to mirror the HTML DRIVER strings checks out for the six Pyodide pages (yh001-yh005_1 each embed DRIVER; yh006/yh006_1 are static result viewers, and index.html explicitly documents that PAMS/parquet dependence makes them non-runnable in the browser — so the coverage gap is intentional and disclosed). I also verified sf.py's fidelity claims: acf and hill_alpha match YH005 analysis._acf / hill_mle_tail_index line-for-line in estimator logic. Remaining gap: nothing references _selftest.py — it is not collected by the workspace tests/ suite, any CI, or a Makefile, so DRIVER/model signature drift (its stated raison d'être) will only be caught if someone remembers to run it manually. Its path-shadowing pattern (each experiment dir prepended to sys.path, module named 'model' re-resolved via importlib.reload) works only in the hardcoded test order; test_yh001 imports 'model' without reload and would silently pick up a stale module if order ever changes.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: Run result: 'playground self-test (real models + sf.py) ... [YH005_1] OK ... ALL OK' (venv) vs 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named numpy' (system python); grep shows _selftest referenced only by itself
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add a slow-marked wrapper in the workspace tests/ (or a CI job using the venv) that shells out to _selftest.py, and give each test an isolated import (importlib.util.spec_from_file_location) instead of sys.path stacking.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Empirical side uses log-returns while all simulators emit simple returns, despite the 'same quantity measured by same code' claim
ideas_research_gaps.detail: market_data.py and jpx_data.py (line 146) compute np.diff(np.log(prices)) for the empirical DiD ground truth, while every adapter computes returns[t] = (P_t - P_{t-1})/P_{t-1}. FINAL_REPORT Key Technical Decision #1 claims 'Same quantity measured by same code… No category error in measurement' — the estimator code is shared, but the input quantity differs by the log vs simple return convention. At daily scale the numeric difference is small for most facts, but skewness and kurtosis (two of the six scored facts) are systematically shifted by the convexity of log(1+r), which matters when deltas of order 0.05 are being sign-scored.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
log_returns = np.diff(np.log(prices), axis=0)  # empirical
returns[t] = (prices[t] - prices[t - 1]) / prices[t - 1]  # all adapters
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Standardize on log-returns end-to-end (convert simulated prices with np.diff(np.log(prices))) and state the convention in the estimator contract.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Eligibility gate is advisory only: INELIGIBLE models are still scored, weighted, and counted in the tensor
ideas_research_gaps.detail: run_cell computes check_eligibility on baseline facts but proceeds unconditionally to compute_matches and apply_mdl_weights; the verdict is only rendered in summaries (TensorOutput.summary lists ineligible cells, but their MATCH/MISMATCH counts and weighted confidences remain in cells/to_dict output). eligibility.py's docstring says an ineligible model's 'intervention-response scoring is unreliable', yet nothing zeroes or excludes those scores, so any downstream aggregation over CellOutput.matches quietly includes them. Note also the gate only checks the fact_ids requested for scoring rather than a fixed realism battery, so a caller scoring a single fact gets a one-check gate.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
eligibility = check_eligibility(adapter_name, baseline_facts)
tracker.record_parameter("eligibility", eligibility.verdict.value)
# --- Score against ground truth ---
matches = compute_matches(model_deltas, ner.ground_truth_deltas)  # unconditional
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Either hard-gate (set confidence to 0 / skip scoring for INELIGIBLE) or multiply confidence_weighted by an eligibility factor, and decouple the gate's fact battery from the scored fact_ids.

ideas_research_gaps.title: history step-0 entry always reports 100% happiness (dataclass default happy=True, recorded before any LLM decision); total_llm_calls also counts no-neighbor short-circuits and errors
ideas_research_gaps.detail: setup() calls _record(0) before any decision is made, so every history series (and history.png) starts at happy_pct=1.0 by dataclass default rather than by measurement — the committed output_v2_ollama_5x5/results.json shows this ({'step': 0, 'happy_pct': 1.0, ...}). src/semantic_schelling.py has the same pattern (_record_state(step=0) in setup with happy defaulting True). Additionally decide_batch returns a DecisionResult even for agents with no neighbors (backend short-circuits without an HTTP call) and for errored calls, yet step() adds len(results) to total_llm_calls, so the reported call count overstates actual LLM usage. Cosmetic in a PoC, but it distorts the first segment of the plotted happiness trajectory and any cost accounting.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
self._record(0)  # setup(), before any backend.decide_batch
-- step() --
results = await self.backend.decide_batch(agent_contexts, neighbors_map)
self.total_llm_calls += len(results)
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Record happiness as None (or run one decision pass) at step 0; count only results whose raw_response is neither '(no neighbors)' nor an ERROR string.

ideas_research_gaps.title: config.yaml references a 'run script' that masks SVXY before 2018-03-01, but no such script exists in the repo; spec T6 extraction to packages/regimes not done
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The vol_complex universe comment says 'the run script masks SVXY before 2018-03-01' (the -1x -> -0.5x leverage change), but grep finds no script in the repository applying that mask; anyone running the vol_complex universe through the standard CLI gets unmasked pre-2018 SVXY, mixing two structurally different instruments in one KDE. Separately, specs/001-monorepo-consolidation.md line 91 maps market-dynamics to packages/regimes + experiments/regimes (milestone T6), which does not exist yet — this imported copy is currently the sole, un-extracted implementation, so fixes here have no canonical twin to drift against (yet).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: # SVXY has a structural change in 2018-02 (-1x → -0.5x); the run script masks SVXY before 2018-03-01.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Either add the masking logic to load_universe/an experiment script, or move the constraint into config (per-ticker start date) so it cannot be forgotten.

ideas_research_gaps.title: On convergence break, the final selected action profile is never executed but is counted in periods_run (off-by-one in period accounting)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: When the cycle check at time t triggers convergence, train() breaks before step_core(actions) runs, yet sets periods = t + 1. The env executed only t periods, so TrainResult.periods_run over-counts by 1, which propagates into the ledger's 'actual' consumption (designmap.run_one_seed: actual = periods_run + burn_in + measure_periods) and into periods_total in the CSV. One period out of >=10^5 is economically negligible, but the ledger is presented as an exact audit artifact (the 0002b reconstruction relied on periods_total being exact), so the accounting convention should at least be stated. The unflushed final transition is already documented in the module docstring; this accounting mismatch is not.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
if stable_checks >= required_checks:
    converged = True
    stable_at = t
    periods = t + 1
    break   # step_core(actions) for period t is never executed
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Set periods = t on the break path (or execute the step before breaking), and note the convention next to _planned_periods.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Cross-mechanism 'paired seed differences' are not common-random-numbers: chunked N=1 draws vs per-period batch draws consume the streams in different orders
ideas_research_gaps.detail: compare_conditions annotates diffs = m - base_m as paired same-seed differences, and run_attribution builds delta_total the same way. Within a mechanism, committed-vs-revisable pairs ARE exact (identical RNG consumption; bcs_per_seed.py's pairing claim is correct for its staleness comparison). But across mechanisms the price/noise streams diverge after the first period: continuous pre-draws jump[8192] then sign[8192] (env._draw_period_n1), while batch-N draws jump[N] then sign[N] per period, so the same master seed yields different realized jump/noise paths. The paired-difference SE is still an unbiased estimate of the SE of the mean difference (it just equals the unpaired variance), so no result is wrong - but the comment implies variance-reduction pairing that does not exist for delta_total/modulation, and a reader auditing se_total might expect correlated paths.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: diffs = m - base_m                    # seed pair diff (same seed vs same seed)  [designmap.py:342] ; env.py:60-76 (chunked) vs env.py:78-89 (per-period) interleave rng_price differently
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Either draw per-period in the continuous path too (making cross-mechanism runs share the per-step jump sequence, enabling true CRN for delta_total), or correct the comments to state that pairing is exact only across staleness within a mechanism.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Incidents 0002a and 0002b are verifiably fixed in code with regression tests (verification requested by the audit brief)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: 0002a (tier-3 key collision): DesignMapPoint now carries eps_beta/gamma/tie_rule and config_hash (SHA-256 over all non-seed fields, designmap.py:46-55, 75); resume matching uses config_hash with a cell-id fallback guarded by a uniqueness check that refuses to resume ambiguous job lists (done_keys designmap.py:495-508, run_design_map.py:167-180); test_designmap.py:126-139 pins that the 9 robustness variants collide on cell_id but not on config_hash. 0002b (ledger lost update): all mutations are serialized via an O_CREAT|O_EXCL lock with reload-under-lock (designmap.py:119-138), the primary record is the append-only journal with rebuild_spent()/verify() (designmap.py:144-176), and tests cover concurrent-instance non-clobbering and snapshot-corruption recovery (test_designmap.py:54-87). Residual weakness is only the snapshot-before-journal write order reported separately.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: d = {k: v for k, v in asdict(cfg).items() if k != "seed"}\nraw = repr(sorted(d.items()))\nreturn hashlib.sha256(raw.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:16]
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: No action needed beyond the journal-ordering fix reported separately.

ideas_research_gaps.title: CI2002.ipynb is a verbatim upstream pams example; committed outputs are from a non-fresh kernel (execution counts 7..12)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: The notebook is a copy of masanorihirano/pams' examples/CI2002.ipynb (the Colab badge still points at the upstream repo), used as the CI2002 baseline reference for YH006. It is properly seeded (SequentialRunner(prng=random.Random(42))) and is valid nbformat-4 JSON with no hardcoded local paths. However, execution counts run 7..12, so the committed outputs were produced mid-session, not by a fresh top-to-bottom run — the stream output (EXECUTION TIME) and the plot cannot be assumed to correspond to the committed cell code. The pip-install cell is commented out, so a fresh environment without pams fails at the import cell. Low risk since it is reference material inside an imported repo, but if it is ever cited as the C1 baseline figure it should be re-executed cleanly.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: cell execution_count sequence: 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12; cell 1: colab badge URL .../masanorihirano/pams/blob/main/examples/CI2002.ipynb; cell 5: prng=random.Random(42)
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Re-run top-to-bottom in a fresh kernel before citing its output, and note in YH006/README that this file is an unmodified upstream example.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Initial history μ0 is the deterministic all-'stay' pattern (K//2), not random as in YH005/Java
ideas_research_gaps.detail: SharedHistoryState defaults mu_init to 5^M // 2, which for M=5 is 1562 = base-5 digits (2,2,2,2,2), i.e., the all-h=0 history — every run and every seed starts from the identical μ. Java draws history = (int)(Math.random()*pattern) and YH005 draws mu0 = rng.integers(0, 5^M). The effect washes out after M endogenous steps and warmup (default 500 steps) is discarded, so impact is negligible, but it is a genuine initialization drift from the reference convention and get_or_init never passes a random mu_init, so the parameter is effectively dead.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: self.mu = mu_init if mu_init is not None else self.K // 2  # deterministic; YH005: mu0 = int(rng.integers(0, K))
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Pass a prng-drawn mu_init in get_or_init (or document the deliberate neutral-history choice next to the μ-lag equivalence note).

ideas_research_gaps.title: Distribution-neutral divergences from the Java reference beyond the known tie-break (initial active index, substitution 'use' handling, review-when-bankrupt ordering)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Three further points where all Python SG implementations (YH005 model/simulate, packages sg, aggregate_sim, YH006 agent) deviate from Sample.java, each distributionally equivalent because strategies are iid uniform, but worth recording so the 'dissertation-faithful' framing is precise: (1) Java initializes use=0 for every player; Pythons draw init_active = rng.integers(0,S) per agent. (2) Java's replace() regenerates recommends/gains/wealth but KEEPS the current `use` slot (and stale openPositions/openPrices); Pythons redraw active_idx uniformly and zero all entry/virtual state on substitution. (3) Java sets alter=true INSTEAD of review when wealth < b at settlement (bankrupt players never review, Sample.java:234-238); Pythons run the strategy review first and then overwrite everything in the bankruptcy branch — the final state is identical, but Python may consume an extra rng.choice draw for an agent about to be substituted, which matters only for Python-side RNG-stream accounting, not for Java parity (no RNG parity is claimed).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: Sample.java: if (wealth < b) { alter = true; } else { review = true; }  ... replace() does not reset `use`.  reference.py steps 8: review always runs for every close, then `if a.w < B:` substitution redraws active_idx.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add a short 'known distribution-neutral deviations from Sample.java' list to the YH005 spec/§7 docstring alongside the already-known tie-break note, so future porting work does not rediscover them.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Verified-true claims (recorded for the fleet) and one unverifiable claim (cap-alloc shim)
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Positive verification results, so other auditors need not re-check: (1) all seven RECORDED constants in tests/test_sg_parity.py match imported/speculation-game-info/experiments/YH005/outputs/baseline_metrics.json and docs/backbone_parity.md exactly; (2) experiments/classical/baseline.py and experiments/speculation_game/baseline.py import only abm_models/stylized_facts — the core-import claim holds; (3) all 5 old GitHub repos (speculation-game-info, PRISM, PROV-ABM-atlas, market-dynamics, ABM-Microstructure) are archived=True with consolidation pointers in their descriptions (AC7 met; agent-based-modeling never had an upstream repo, consistent with README's snapshot note); (4) ADR 0001's 'PRISM 版と PROV-ABM 版は互いに byte 同一 (import パスのみ差)' is exactly right — diff is a single import statement; (5) subtree merges preserved upstream commit DAGs (e.g. speculation-game-info parent aad2c74 with its PR history); (6) suite size matches the briefing: 74 collected, 73 fast + 1 slow. NOT verifiable from this session: spec line 20-21's O5 claim that capital-allocation has a validated 'use-shared-provenance' shim branch — the capital-allocation repo is outside this session's GitHub access (it exists and is unarchived, pushed 2026-06-13), so that claim rests on the spec's word alone.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: baseline_metrics.json std=0.0032560438166104556 == tests/test_sg_parity.py RECORDED['std']; GitHub API: PRISM archived:True desc '[Consolidated→financial-abm-lab] imported/PRISM' (same pattern for the other four); diff PRISM/src/prism/adapters/sg.py vs PROV-ABM-atlas/toy/models/sg.py → 'from prism.types' vs 'from toy.models.types' only.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: For the one unverifiable claim, add the cap-alloc shim commit hash or PR link to spec 001's O5 bullet so it is auditable without repo access.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Static sweep otherwise clean: no SyntaxErrors, no F821/F811/E711/E712 anywhere; residual ruff hits are hygiene-class only
ideas_research_gaps.detail: compileall over packages/, src/, experiments/ and imported/speculation-game-info/ exits 0 with no output (no SyntaxErrors). Repo-wide ruff for the correctness-relevant classes (F821 undefined name, F811 redefinition, E711/E712 comparison bugs, F632) passes everywhere, including all six imported repos. The 31 workspace hits and 78 F-class hits in speculation-game-info are all F401 unused imports in scripts (none in __init__ re-export positions, so no API promises broken), F541 empty f-strings, F841 dead locals and E702 semicolons. The single dead local worth noting in canonical model code is gcmg/model.py:139 `N = actions.size` — computed and never used inside GCMG tick(); it is harmless (A_count/n_active are what get logged) but is dead weight in a canonical core. The F841 `exc` in fingerprint_atlas/propose_cli.py:241 was verified benign (the except block re-raises after updating proposal status).
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
packages/abm_models/abm_models/gcmg/model.py:139: N = actions.size  (never read; lines 140-143 use A_count/n_active/winning_side)
$ uv run python -m compileall -q packages/ src/ experiments/ imported/speculation-game-info/ → exit 0, empty output
$ uv run ruff check . --select F821,F811,E711,E712 → All checks passed!
speculation-game-info F-code census: 31 F401, 32 F541, 15 F841, zero F821/F811.
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Drop the dead `N = actions.size` line; optionally run `ruff --fix` for the 18 auto-fixable workspace hygiene hits, keeping imported/ untouched to preserve subtree history fidelity.

ideas_research_gaps.title: LuxMarchesi result dict embeds the Params dataclass object, breaking result-dict uniformity
ideas_research_gaps.detail: simulate() returns {"params": params, ...} where params is the Params dataclass instance — the only model in the REGISTRY whose result dict contains a non-array Python object (verified at runtime: np.asarray(result['params']) yields dtype=object). Every other model returns pure ndarray/scalar values. Any generic persistence layer (np.savez, HDF5, parquet) or hashing/fingerprinting over REGISTRY results must special-case this key; a naive np.asarray-based canonicalizer had to fall back to repr() for it during this audit. The value is also redundant — the caller supplied the params.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
return {
    "prices": prices,
    "returns": returns,
    "z": z_series,
    "x": x_series,
    "params": params,
    "zbar": compute_zbar(params),
}
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Return dataclasses.asdict(params) (plain scalars) or drop the key from the run() wrapper's result to keep the ABMModel result contract array-only.

ideas_research_gaps.title: Runtime verification: all 8 REGISTRY models are deterministic, seed-sensitive, and protocol-conformant; SG backend parity is bit-exact
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Positive empirical result for the audit record. For each of the 8 registered models: (a) two runs with the same seed are bit-identical across every result key even when the global random.seed/np.random.seed state is perturbed between runs — no model touches legacy global RNG; (b) a different seed changes every stochastic output key (zbar and params for lux_marchesi are correctly seed-independent analytic/config values); (c) same-instance reuse across runs is bit-identical (no hidden state); (d) isinstance(model, ABMModel) holds and name matches the registry key for all 8. tests/test_sg_parity.py: 2 passed in 38.5s. SG vectorized vs reference at N=1000/M=5/S=2/T=2000, seed=7: all 14 result keys bit-identical including dtype, and the nested round_trips dict (418,001 records across 6 arrays) is byte-identical — the docs/backbone_parity.md claim of exact SG parity is confirmed at this scale, not just at the tested baseline.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence: def run(self, *, seed: int) -> dict[str, Any]: ...  # all 8 REGISTRY classes conform; empirical: determinism OK, seed sensitivity OK, instance-reuse OK for all models

ideas_research_gaps.title: Broken scripts and green tests coexist because only library-path code is tested; standalone entry points (smoke, calibrate, profile) have zero test coverage
ideas_research_gaps.detail: Empirical status summary for the lane: GREEN — pams importable via proxy (0.2.2, equals the requirements pin, no API drift); YH006 tests 13/13 pass; YH005 tests 18/18 pass; run_experiment.run_condition() verified working end-to-end on a tiny C3 (238 round-trips, T=80); aggregate_sim.py and all analysis modules import cleanly; run_aggregate_c0.py completes standalone. BROKEN — smoke_test.py, calibrate_c_ticks.py, profile_c3_plan_a.py (all: `class for MMFCNAgent is found 0 times`). The tests are correct precisely where the scripts are wrong (tests/test_parity.py:43-44 registers both SpeculationAgent and MMFCNAgent), which shows the MMFCNAgent migration updated tested code paths and missed everything the suite does not touch. A single parametrized 'entry points at tiny scale' test (smoke config with ~20 steps) would have caught all three regressions.
ideas_research_gaps.evidence:
runner.class_register(SpeculationAgent)
    runner.class_register(MMFCNAgent)   # tests do it right; smoke_test.py:47, calibrate_c_ticks.py:44, profile_c3_plan_a.py:38 do not
ideas_research_gaps.suggestion: Add a fast test that imports and executes each standalone script's main path with tiny parameters (steps<=50, agents<=10), so config/registration drift breaks CI instead of the researcher's next session.

refuted_by_verification.title: returns_of silently converts non-positive prices to NaN, poisoning downstream statistics without warning
Both scheduled workflows open PRs against a nonexistent ephemeral session branch, breaking PR creation and the snapshot accumulation loop
refuted_by_verification.why: The finding's central factual premise is wrong. The auditor relied on stale local refs ('git branch -a shows only main and claude/speculation-game-cleanup-4inq82'), but `git ls-remote --heads origin` shows the branch claude/vibrant-ptolemy-fapuxw DOES exist on the remote (9ca0395850ef...). So `base: claude/vibrant-ptolemy-fapuxw` in the checked-out copies of .github/workflows/ingest_arxiv.yml:127 and propose.yml:86 does not point to a nonexistent branch, and PR creation does not fail for that reason. Direct evidence the pipeline actually works: remote head branches ingest-arxiv/28287737193, ingest-arxiv/28309101263 (both containing data/literature_methods.json) and propose/28374237859 exist — these are exactly the `branch: ingest-arxiv/${{github.run_id}}` PR head branches the workflows create, so create-pull-request has succeeded on real runs. Additional refuting facts: (1) the two workflow files do not exist on main at all (`git show main:.github/workflows/ingest_arxiv.yml` fails), so the claimed 'every scheduled run fails' scenario cannot occur — scheduled workflows only fire from the default branch (main), where they are absent; runs happen via workflow_dispatch on the vibrant-ptolemy branch. (2) The live/newer revision of the workflow on claude/vibrant-ptolemy-fapuxw already sets `base: main` (line 149 there); the copy on the current cleanup branch is simply an older snapshot of that file. (3) The 'accumulation never round-trips' evidence (snapshot untracked everywhere) is explained by the snapshot PRs simply not having been merged yet, not by a broken base. Residual truth: if the cleanup branch's stale workflow copy were merged to main verbatim, PRs would target a leftover session branch instead of main — a real but low-impact hygiene issue (the fix already exists upstream), which does not match the reported failure mode or major severity.

refuted_by_verification.title: RNG contract §7.2 places the exogenous μ redraw 'at step end', but both implementations draw it mid-step before review/bankruptcy draws
refuted_by_verification.why: The finding's factual premise — that §7.2 orders the exogenous μ redraw AFTER the review/bankruptcy draws — misreads the document. In YH005_PLAN.md §7.2 the contract is a sequential list of phases: 意思決定フェーズ (decision draws) → 履歴更新フェーズ (the μ redraw paragraph, line ~615) → then the close-review/bankruptcy paragraph. Read top-to-bottom, §7.2 places the μ redraw BEFORE the review rng.choice and bankruptcy draws, which is exactly what both implementations do (model.py:186 draws μ at processing step 4/5; review/bankruptcy draws follow at the step-8 loop, model.py ~line 250). The phrase 「step 末に **1 回**」 is loose wording (it primarily contrasts with per-agent consumption: once per step, not N times), but it cannot induce the wrong order because (a) the very next paragraph describing review/bankruptcy is itself introduced with 「ステップ末、エージェント index 順に」 — both are 'step end', so the only ordering information is the paragraph sequence, which is correct; and (b) the same document's §3.11 「ステップ内の処理順 (厳守)」 explicitly pins the exogenous redraw at numbered step 5, before step 9 (review → bankruptcy), and §7.2's opening frames the contract per that processing order. A reimplementer following the contract verbatim gets the correct RNG consumption order; the hypothesized parity break requires reading 'step 末' against both the paragraph order and §3.11, which is not a literal reading. Parity tests (§7.4/§8.1, including the Null A exogenous case) further guard the two existing implementations. At most this is a wording-polish suggestion ('step 末' could say 'μ 更新時 (§3.11 step 5)'), not docs-drift; the doc and code do not disagree on order.

refuted_by_verification.title: compare_figure reimplements the Hill estimator and hold-ratio aggregation inline despite SPEC §9's 'no new analysis functions' rule; N_est heuristic biases idle share
refuted_by_verification.why:
The two substantive defect claims do not hold up; what remains is documented, intentional code duplication with no numerical consequence.

1. N_est bias claim — empirically false at the actual run configuration. I ran the actual simulator (aggregate_sim.simulate_aggregate, N=100, T=50000, the config stated in the figure titles, including the actual seed 777): the per-step sum num_buy+num_sell+num_active_hold+num_passive_hold reaches exactly 100 at 22 steps (seed 777; 23 and 39 steps for seeds 42 and 1). So N_est = max(...) recovers the true N exactly and the idle share is not deformed. With 50000 steps, the estimator saturates in practice.

2. The N_est heuristic is not a compare_figure invention that diverges from the shared layer — it is copied verbatim from the shared layer itself: YH005/analysis.py plot_hold_ratio, lines 316-320, uses the identical `N_est = int((num_buy + num_sell + num_act + num_pas).max())` with the same np.clip idle derivation and even a comment explaining the choice. So the compare figures are like-for-like consistent with every YH005 baseline figure. Moreover, the finding's proposed fix (`_meta['N_sg']`) is unavailable for two of the five conditions: the aggregate-side pkls (c0u/c0p) come from aggregate_sim.py, whose result dict (lines ~278-293) contains no `_meta` key at all — only the LOB adapter (yh006_to_yh005_adapter.py:217) emits `_meta`. The auditor misread which pkls carry that metadata.

3. Hill estimator "drift" claim — the inline raw-branch code (compare_figure.py:157-166) is line-for-line semantically identical to YH005/analysis.py:_hill_alpha_xmin_percentile (lines 140-154): xmin = 90th percentile, tail = x >= xmin, alpha = 1/mean(log(tail/xmin)). There is no divergence today (same xmin convention, same tail inclusion); the claimed drift is hypothetical. Note also that the YH005 function is a private (underscore-prefixed) helper in a sibling experiment directory that SPEC §9(1) forbids touching, so importing it is not cleanly available either; the file header (lines 21-22) shows the author was aware of §9 and considered the panel plotting code compliant.

Residual truth: there is real duplication of an estimator formula, which is a style/maintenance observation, not a defect — no wrong numbers are produced, comparisons are internally consistent, and the metrics JSON is figure-support output, not canonical results. One caveat I could not check empirically: whether the LOB conditions (C2/C3, requiring PAMS runs) also saturate N_est; but for those pkls `_meta['N_sg']` does exist, and any shortfall would only affect a presentation figure, and the same heuristic governs the canonical YH005 layer anyway.

refuted_by_verification.title: sliced_wasserstein truncates both sorted samples to the shorter length — not a Wasserstein distance when sample sizes differ
refuted_by_verification.why: The code reading is accurate: sliced_wasserstein (imported/PROV-ABM-atlas/toy/calibration.py:82-85) truncates both sorted arrays to min length, which would misalign quantiles for unequal sizes. However, the mismatched-size path is unreachable: sliced_wasserstein is called only at calibration.py:116 and :181, and in both calibrate_sf_equivalent and calibrate_search the ref and h_dist arrays are produced by sf_distribution with the identical n_runs parameter of the same function call — there is no API path that yields different row counts for ref and other. External callers (experiments/runners/equivalence_check.py, experiments/runners/calibrate_search.py) invoke only the calibrate_* wrappers, never sliced_wasserstein directly. The finding itself concedes the bug is 'latent' and only bites under a hypothetical future asymmetric use. Under the refutation criteria (path unreachable / fully guarded by callers), this is at most a robustness note, not a defect in behavior. Additionally the code is in imported/, a dogfood toy project, not canonical package code.

critic_assessment: The fleet audit is deep on code correctness, statistics, RNG/reproducibility, test quality, and doc drift — but it audited exactly one snapshot: the checked-out worktree. Whole defect classes were never hunted, and I verified concrete evidence for each gap. (1) Off-main branches: the audit never looked at branch topology. origin/claude/yh007-policy-overhaul-h1rya4 carries 34 unmerged commits of active YH007-8 research (including a specs/003 that the spec-drift lane, which declared specs/ ground truth, never saw); origin/propose/28374237859 is 57 commits ahead with unaudited fingerprint_atlas code (dashboard.py, techniques.py 390 lines, an idea_cli/idea_judge 'ideas pipeline') plus CI-auto-committed LLM output (proposals/*.md); ingest-arxiv/* branches hold the data/literature_methods.json snapshot whose absence the propose.yml finding complains about; and local HEAD and origin/main have diverged 1-vs-1, so it is ambiguous which state the audit even certified. Roughly 10k lines of the newest research/LLM code were read by nobody. (2) Licensing/copyright: no lane examined it. The root repo has no LICENSE; imported/speculation-game-info/LICENSE literally says 'Copyright (c) 2026 [YOUR NAME]'; 10 third-party publisher PDFs (Cont-Bouchaud 1997, Lux-Marchesi 2000, three Katahira papers, Jefferies 2001, etc., ~1.2 MB) plus Katahira's dissertation Java code are committed and redistributed; and PRISM MIT code was extracted into packages/ without checking attribution/license metadata in packages/*/pyproject.toml. (3) Secrets: I scanned the working tree for common token patterns (clean), but the 1.74 GiB git history — including CI-generated branches from workflows holding GROQ_API_KEY with contents:write — was never scanned by any lane, and nobody audited whether workflow steps can leak the secret into logs/PR bodies. (4) Concurrency outside YH006_1 multiprocessing was hunted nowhere: two scheduled workflows rebuild the same SQLite DB and push branches on independent cron schedules, and db.py's transaction/locking discipline is unexamined. (5) Systematic data-artifact integrity: 2520 parquet + 58 PNG + 55 JSON + 10 PDF tracked files were never bulk-validated; my spot check found 39 tracked zero-byte files (some known, some not: imported/market-dynamics/KICKOFF_PROMPT.md, PRISM MISSION_COMPLETE, three empty YH00x memo.txt) and two committed non-UTF8 .log.err runtime logs in YH006_1/logs — committed logs being their own hygiene class nobody flagged. Unicode/encoding and platform assumptions I checked myself and consider low-risk (Linux-only researcher, json ensure_ascii, only 2 non-UTF8 files). The five probes below cover the genuinely unexamined surface.
